• Show All Code
  • Hide All Code

Case Study 3: Structural Equation Modelling

Brand Equity Drivers at Galeries Lafayette Berlin

Giovanni Carta, Zahra Ghafghazi, Sophia Ropelewski

2026-05-06

0. Setup & Data Loading

Loads all R packages needed for EFA, CFA, and SEM.

library(tidyverse)
library(psych)
library(lavaan)
library(semPlot)
library(GPArotation)
library(corrplot)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
library(tibble)

We first load the data.

data_raw <- read_csv("Case Study III_Structural Equation Modeling.csv")
cat("Dataset dimensions:", nrow(data_raw), "rows x", ncol(data_raw), "columns\n")
## Dataset dimensions: 553 rows x 45 columns

When inspecting the data, we see that there are 999 values present. We decide to replace all 999 with NA so they are excluded from calculations, handled later by the model.

data_raw <- data_raw %>% mutate(across(everything(), ~ na_if(., 999)))

na_counts <- colSums(is.na(data_raw))
na_pct    <- round(na_counts / nrow(data_raw) * 100, 1)

data.frame(Variable = names(na_counts), N_Missing = na_counts, Pct = na_pct) %>%
  filter(N_Missing > 0) %>% arrange(desc(Pct)) %>%
  kbl(caption = "Missing values per variable", row.names = FALSE) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"), full_width = FALSE)
Missing values per variable
Variable N_Missing Pct
SAT_3 40 7.2
TRU_2 33 6.0
C_CR2 30 5.4
Im5 29 5.2
Im18 28 5.1
TRU_1 28 5.1
TRU_3 28 5.1
Im7 26 4.7
Im14 24 4.3
Im16 24 4.3
Im12 21 3.8
Im3 20 3.6
C_CR1 20 3.6
Im2 18 3.3
C_REP3 18 3.3
COM_A3 18 3.3
Im22 17 3.1
Im9 16 2.9
C_REP2 16 2.9
SAT_P2 16 2.9
Im13 15 2.7
Im1 14 2.5
COM_A1 14 2.5
SAT_P4 13 2.4
SAT_P6 13 2.4
Im11 12 2.2
Im15 12 2.2
Im17 12 2.2
Im19 12 2.2
COM_A2 11 2.0
Im4 10 1.8
C_CR4 10 1.8
SAT_2 10 1.8
Im6 9 1.6
Im20 9 1.6
COM_A4 9 1.6
SAT_P1 8 1.4
SAT_P5 8 1.4
Im8 6 1.1
Im10 6 1.1
C_CR3 6 1.1
SAT_P3 6 1.1
Im21 5 0.9
C_REP1 5 0.9
SAT_1 5 0.9

Doing a more throughout check, we see that missing values are present in several variables, with a maximum of 7.2% missing in SAT_3. All models use missing = "fiml" (Full Information Maximum Likelihood), which retains all 553 observations without list-wise deletion or imputation. FIML is the recommended approach for SEM with incomplete data.

image_data <- data_raw %>% select(Im1:Im22)

1. Exploratory Factor Analysis (EFA)

1.1 Pre-tests: KMO and Bartlett

Next, we need to check if we can apply factor analysis for the data. therefore, we check the following: KMO must be > 0.70; Bartlett must reject H0 (p < 0.05).

## factorability checks
cor_image <- cor(image_data, use = "pairwise.complete.obs")

kmo_result <- KMO(cor_image)
cat("Overall KMO (MSA):", round(kmo_result$MSA, 3), "\n")
## Overall KMO (MSA): 0.881
bart <- cortest.bartlett(cor_image, n = nrow(na.omit(image_data)))
cat("Bartlett Chi2:", round(bart$chisq, 2), "| df:", bart$df,
    "| p:", format(bart$p.value, scientific = TRUE), "\n")
## Bartlett Chi2: 6283.04 | df: 231 | p: 0e+00
corrplot(cor_image, method = "color", type = "upper", tl.cex = 0.72,
         col  = colorRampPalette(c("#c0392b","white","#2980b9"))(200),
         title = "Pairwise Correlation Matrix - Image Items Im1-Im22",
         mar   = c(0, 0, 2, 0))

The overall KMO (MSA) is 0.881, indicating good factorability. Bartlett’s test is highly significant (p < 0.001), confirming that the correlation matrix is not an identity matrix. Both tests confirm that EFA is appropriate for this data.

1.2 Factor Retention

Now we need to get an idea of the number of factors for our analysis. Therefore, we compute eigenvalues, scree plot and runs parallel analysis to determine how many factors to extract.

#kaiser
eigen_vals <- eigen(cor_image)$values

data.frame(Factor = seq_along(eigen_vals),
           Eigenvalue = round(eigen_vals, 3),
           Cum_Var_Pct = round(cumsum(eigen_vals) / sum(eigen_vals) * 100, 1)) %>%
  kbl(caption = "Eigenvalues and cumulative variance explained") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
  row_spec(which(eigen_vals >= 1), bold = TRUE, background = "#d6eaf8")
Eigenvalues and cumulative variance explained
Factor Eigenvalue Cum_Var_Pct
1 8.994 40.9
2 2.384 51.7
3 1.522 58.6
4 1.409 65.0
5 1.264 70.8
6 1.116 75.9
7 0.787 79.4
8 0.720 82.7
9 0.568 85.3
10 0.483 87.5
11 0.387 89.2
12 0.350 90.8
13 0.334 92.3
14 0.306 93.7
15 0.289 95.1
16 0.231 96.1
17 0.222 97.1
18 0.183 97.9
19 0.157 98.7
20 0.115 99.2
21 0.100 99.6
22 0.080 100.0 
#scree plot
ev_df <- data.frame(Factor = seq_along(eigen_vals), EV = eigen_vals)

ggplot(ev_df, aes(x = Factor, y = EV)) +
  geom_line(color = "#2980b9", linewidth = 1.1) +
  geom_point(aes(color = EV > 1), size = 3.5) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", color = "#c0392b", linewidth = 0.8) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("FALSE" = "grey70", "TRUE" = "#e74c3c"),
                     labels = c("FALSE" = "EV < 1", "TRUE" = "EV >= 1 (Kaiser)"), name = NULL) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:22) +
  labs(title = "Scree Plot - 22 Image Items",
       subtitle = "Red dashed line = Kaiser criterion (EV = 1)",
       x = "Factor Number", y = "Eigenvalue") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "top", panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))

# parallel analysis
fa.parallel(image_data, fa = "fa", fm = "minres", n.iter = 5,
            main = "Parallel Analysis - Factor Retention")

## Parallel analysis suggests that the number of factors =  7  and the number of components =  NA

The Kaiser criterion (eigenvalue > 1) here points to 6 factors, parallel analysis recommends 7-8. We try different solutions in the next part.

1.3 Varimax Exploration (Orthogonal Rotation)

We first inspect multiple solutions with Varimax (orthogonal) rotation across different numbers of factors. Varimax assumes factors are uncorrelated. We try out : 5, 6, 7, 8 and 9 factors solution.

# trying out several solutions
efa_5_v <- fa.sort(fa(image_data, nfactors = 5, rotate = "varimax", fm = "pa", use = "pairwise"))
efa_6_v <- fa.sort(fa(image_data, nfactors = 6, rotate = "varimax", fm = "pa", use = "pairwise"))
efa_7_v <- fa.sort(fa(image_data, nfactors = 7, rotate = "varimax", fm = "pa", use = "pairwise"))
efa_8_v <- fa.sort(fa(image_data, nfactors = 8, rotate = "varimax", fm = "pa", use = "pairwise"))
efa_9_v <- fa.sort(fa(image_data, nfactors = 9, rotate = "varimax", fm = "pa", use = "pairwise"))

cat("=== 5-Factor Varimax ===\n"); print(efa_5_v$loadings, cutoff = 0.30)
## === 5-Factor Varimax ===
## 
## Loadings:
##      PA2   PA1   PA4   PA3   PA5  
## Im7  0.808                        
## Im8  0.805                        
## Im10 0.727                        
## Im14 0.718                        
## Im6  0.685                        
## Im9  0.463                   0.331
## Im1        0.857                  
## Im2        0.852                  
## Im15       0.594             0.309
## Im16       0.465 0.355            
## Im19       0.450 0.398            
## Im17       0.403 0.311 0.333 0.376
## Im18       0.331 0.312 0.315 0.323
## Im4              0.888            
## Im3              0.850            
## Im5              0.646            
## Im22                   0.805      
## Im20                   0.792      
## Im21                   0.697      
## Im12                         0.823
## Im13                         0.745
## Im11                         0.535
## 
##                  PA2   PA1   PA4   PA3   PA5
## SS loadings    3.526 2.938 2.801 2.390 2.378
## Proportion Var 0.160 0.134 0.127 0.109 0.108
## Cumulative Var 0.160 0.294 0.421 0.530 0.638
cat("\n=== 6-Factor Varimax ===\n"); print(efa_6_v$loadings, cutoff = 0.30)
## 
## === 6-Factor Varimax ===
## 
## Loadings:
##      PA2    PA1    PA4    PA5    PA3    PA6   
## Im7   0.807                              0.329
## Im8   0.779                                   
## Im10  0.750                                   
## Im14  0.735                                   
## Im6   0.703                              0.415
## Im9   0.442                0.318         0.326
## Im1          0.857                            
## Im2          0.852                            
## Im15         0.588         0.302              
## Im16         0.467  0.364                     
## Im19         0.448  0.405                     
## Im4                 0.891                     
## Im3                 0.851                     
## Im5                 0.650                     
## Im12                       0.808              
## Im13                       0.727              
## Im11                       0.550              
## Im20                              0.835       
## Im22                              0.776       
## Im21                              0.715       
## Im17         0.377  0.302  0.357         0.489
## Im18         0.301  0.305  0.301         0.481
## 
##                  PA2   PA1   PA4   PA5   PA3   PA6
## SS loadings    3.439 2.894 2.853 2.353 2.307 1.108
## Proportion Var 0.156 0.132 0.130 0.107 0.105 0.050
## Cumulative Var 0.156 0.288 0.418 0.524 0.629 0.680
cat("\n=== 7-Factor Varimax ===\n"); print(efa_7_v$loadings, cutoff = 0.30)
## 
## === 7-Factor Varimax ===
## 
## Loadings:
##      PA4   PA1   PA2   PA3   PA5   PA7   PA6  
## Im4  0.907                                    
## Im3  0.843                                    
## Im5  0.642                                    
## Im1        0.865                              
## Im2        0.852                              
## Im15       0.572                              
## Im16 0.344 0.436                              
## Im19 0.383 0.415                              
## Im7              0.886                        
## Im6              0.796                        
## Im8              0.555             0.532      
## Im9              0.464       0.325            
## Im20                   0.836                  
## Im22                   0.770                  
## Im21                   0.712                  
## Im12                         0.862            
## Im13                         0.712            
## Im11                         0.534            
## Im10                               0.864      
## Im14             0.307             0.786      
## Im17                                     0.814
## Im18                                     0.726
## 
##                  PA4   PA1   PA2   PA3   PA5   PA7   PA6
## SS loadings    2.719 2.665 2.339 2.261 2.167 2.048 1.611
## Proportion Var 0.124 0.121 0.106 0.103 0.098 0.093 0.073
## Cumulative Var 0.124 0.245 0.351 0.454 0.552 0.645 0.719
cat("\n=== 8-Factor Varimax ===\n"); print(efa_8_v$loadings, cutoff = 0.30)
## 
## === 8-Factor Varimax ===
## 
## Loadings:
##      PA4   PA2   PA3   PA5   PA1   PA7   PA6   PA8  
## Im4  0.901                                          
## Im3  0.830                                          
## Im5  0.650                                          
## Im7        0.889                                    
## Im6        0.797                                    
## Im8        0.569                   0.502            
## Im9        0.469       0.328                        
## Im20             0.839                              
## Im22             0.772                              
## Im21             0.715                              
## Im12                   0.864                        
## Im13                   0.718                        
## Im11                   0.535                        
## Im1                          0.881                  
## Im2                          0.837                  
## Im15                   0.311 0.465             0.369
## Im14       0.303                   0.831            
## Im10       0.305                   0.828            
## Im17                                     0.803      
## Im18                                     0.747      
## Im16                                           0.771
## Im19 0.318                                     0.505
## 
##                  PA4   PA2   PA3   PA5   PA1   PA7   PA6   PA8
## SS loadings    2.545 2.391 2.296 2.221 2.150 1.949 1.617 1.263
## Proportion Var 0.116 0.109 0.104 0.101 0.098 0.089 0.074 0.057
## Cumulative Var 0.116 0.224 0.329 0.430 0.527 0.616 0.690 0.747
cat("\n=== 9-Factor Varimax ===\n"); print(efa_9_v$loadings, cutoff = 0.30)
## 
## === 9-Factor Varimax ===
## 
## Loadings:
##      PA4    PA3    PA2    PA5    PA1    PA7    PA6    PA8    PA9   
## Im4   0.902                                                        
## Im3   0.833                                                        
## Im5   0.652                                                        
## Im20         0.833                                                 
## Im22         0.775                                                 
## Im21         0.723                                                 
## Im7                 0.886                                          
## Im6                 0.790                                          
## Im8                 0.561                0.510                     
## Im9                 0.457  0.322                              0.439
## Im12                       0.878                                   
## Im13                       0.711                                   
## Im11                       0.525                                   
## Im1                               0.881                            
## Im2                               0.841                            
## Im15                       0.304  0.468                0.364       
## Im14                                     0.839                     
## Im10                                     0.836                     
## Im17                                            0.841              
## Im18                                            0.717              
## Im16                                                   0.783       
## Im19  0.320                                            0.503       
## 
##                  PA4   PA3   PA2   PA5   PA1   PA7   PA6   PA8   PA9
## SS loadings    2.570 2.306 2.303 2.184 2.168 2.006 1.647 1.269 0.268
## Proportion Var 0.117 0.105 0.105 0.099 0.099 0.091 0.075 0.058 0.012
## Cumulative Var 0.117 0.222 0.326 0.426 0.524 0.615 0.690 0.748 0.760

Summary of Varimax solutions:

  • 5 factors: substantial cross-loadings throughout, shows clear under-factoring.
  • 6 factors: still several problematic cross-loaders (Im17, Im18, Im15, Im9, Im6, Im7, Im16, Im19).
  • 7 factors: cleaner structure: each factor has at least 2 items loading distinctly. Residual cross-loaders: Im16, Im19 (low main loadings), Im8, Im9.
  • 8 factors: the eighth factor is defined by a single item with two weak secondary loaders, could suggest over-factoring.
  • 9 factors: only one item loads cleanly on the ninth factor: overfactoring.

The 7-factor solution provides the best balance of parsimony and interpretability and is retained for further refinement.

# comparing the tried out solutions
compare_efa_quant <- function(fa_obj, nf) {
  ld <- unclass(fa_obj$loadings)
  abs_ld <- abs(ld)
  primary <- apply(abs_ld, 1, max)
  cross   <- apply(abs_ld, 1, function(x) sort(x, decreasing = TRUE)[2] > 0.30)
  vc <- fa_obj$Vaccounted
  data.frame(
    N_Factors        = nf,
    Cum_Var_Pct      = round(vc[3, ncol(vc)] * 100, 1),
    Mean_Communality = round(mean(fa_obj$communality), 3),
    Items_h2_lt_040  = sum(fa_obj$communality < 0.40),
    Items_Weak_Prim  = sum(primary < 0.40),
    Cross_Loaders    = sum(cross)
  )
}

efa_quant_compare <- bind_rows(
  compare_efa_quant(efa_5_v, 5),
  compare_efa_quant(efa_6_v, 6),
  compare_efa_quant(efa_7_v, 7),
  compare_efa_quant(efa_8_v, 8)
)

efa_quant_compare %>%
  kbl(caption = "Quantitative comparison of 5, 6, 7, 8 factor Varimax solutions",
      col.names = c("N Factors","Cum Var %","Mean h²",
                    "Items h²<0.40","Weak primary","Cross-loaders")) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
  row_spec(3, bold = TRUE, background = "#d5f5e3")
Quantitative comparison of 5, 6, 7, 8 factor Varimax solutions
N Factors Cum Var % Mean h² Items h²<0.40 Weak primary Cross-loaders
5 63.8 0.638 1 1 6
6 68.0 0.680 0 0 8
7 71.9 0.719 1 0 5
8 74.7 0.747 1 0 6

1.3.1 Item Refinement — Step 1: Remove Im16 and Im19

Im16 (“Professional Appearance Towards Customers”) and Im19 (“Professional Organisation”) both exhibit low primary loadings (< 0.45) and cross-loadings. This might suggest that they do not belong to a single clear dimension. The decision is taken to remove them.

# remove 16 and 19
image_data_A <- image_data %>% select(-Im16, -Im19)

efa_7_A <- fa.sort(fa(image_data_A, nfactors = 7, rotate = "varimax",
                      fm = "pa", use = "pairwise"))
cat("=== 7-Factor Varimax: after removing Im16 & Im19 ===\n")
## === 7-Factor Varimax: after removing Im16 & Im19 ===
print(efa_7_A$loadings, cutoff = 0.30)
## 
## Loadings:
##      PA4   PA2   PA3   PA1   PA5   PA7   PA6  
## Im4  0.910                                    
## Im3  0.841                                    
## Im5  0.650                                    
## Im7        0.882                              
## Im6        0.799                              
## Im8        0.564                   0.520      
## Im9        0.468       0.330                  
## Im20             0.840                        
## Im22             0.772                        
## Im21             0.716                        
## Im12                   0.866                  
## Im13                   0.717                  
## Im11                   0.536                  
## Im1                          0.882            
## Im2                          0.864            
## Im15                   0.321 0.508            
## Im10                               0.846      
## Im14                               0.826      
## Im17                                     0.794
## Im18                                     0.763
## 
##                  PA4   PA2   PA3   PA1   PA5   PA7   PA6
## SS loadings    2.458 2.333 2.258 2.176 2.170 1.962 1.587
## Proportion Var 0.123 0.117 0.113 0.109 0.108 0.098 0.079
## Cumulative Var 0.123 0.240 0.352 0.461 0.570 0.668 0.747

1.3.2 Item Refinement — Step 2: Remove Im8 (keeping Im16, Im19 already gone)

Im8 (“Expertise in French Traditional Cuisine”) continues to show similar loadings across two factors, making factor assignment ambiguous. We remove it and check the resulting structure with 7 factors.

# remove 8
image_data_B <- image_data %>% select(-Im16, -Im19, -Im8)

efa_7_B <- fa.sort(fa(image_data_B, nfactors = 7, rotate = "varimax",
                      fm = "pa", use = "pairwise"))
cat("=== 7-Factor Varimax: after removing Im16, Im19 & Im8 ===\n")
## === 7-Factor Varimax: after removing Im16, Im19 & Im8 ===
print(efa_7_B$loadings, cutoff = 0.30)
## 
## Loadings:
##      PA4   PA3   PA1   PA5   PA2   PA7   PA6  
## Im4  0.912                                    
## Im3  0.843                                    
## Im5  0.652                                    
## Im20       0.841                              
## Im22       0.772                              
## Im21       0.716                              
## Im12             0.866                        
## Im13             0.717                        
## Im11             0.538                        
## Im1                    0.883                  
## Im2                    0.863                  
## Im15             0.323 0.508                  
## Im7                          0.848            
## Im6                          0.824            
## Im9              0.335       0.470            
## Im10                               0.837      
## Im14                               0.833      
## Im17                                     0.803
## Im18                                     0.755
## 
##                  PA4   PA3   PA1   PA5   PA2   PA7   PA6
## SS loadings    2.457 2.252 2.174 2.165 1.972 1.683 1.588
## Proportion Var 0.129 0.119 0.114 0.114 0.104 0.089 0.084
## Cumulative Var 0.129 0.248 0.362 0.476 0.580 0.669 0.752

The structure is considerably cleaner without Im16, Im19 and Im8. Im9 (“French Fashion”) still shows a modest loading on the French Identity factor with some secondary loading, and will be evaluated more carefully in the oblique rotation comparison below.

1.4 Final EFA: Oblique Rotation (Oblimin)

Since image perceptions in a retail context are conceptually related (e.g., luxury and decoration overlap), we expect the factors to correlate. An oblique Oblimin rotation is therefore more appropriate than Varimax, as it relaxes the independence assumption and yields a cleaner pattern matrix for correlated constructs.

We compare two candidate item sets to decide whether to retain or drop Im9:

  • Candidate A: remove Im16, Im19, Im8, Im9
  • Candidate B (chosen): remove Im16, Im19, Im8 but retain Im9
# Candidate A: remove Im16, Im19, Im8, Im9
image_data_noIm9 <- image_data %>% select(-Im16, -Im19, -Im8, -Im9)

efa_7_obA <- fa.sort(fa(image_data_noIm9, nfactors = 7, rotate = "oblimin",
                        fm = "minres", use = "pairwise"))

cat("=== Candidate A: 7-Factor Oblimin, -Im16/-Im19/-Im8/-Im9 ===\n")
## === Candidate A: 7-Factor Oblimin, -Im16/-Im19/-Im8/-Im9 ===
print(efa_7_obA$loadings, cutoff = 0.30)
## 
## Loadings:
##      MR4    MR3    MR1    MR2    MR5    MR6    MR7   
## Im4   1.007                                          
## Im3   0.911                                          
## Im5   0.679                                          
## Im20         0.917                                   
## Im22         0.792                                   
## Im21         0.745                                   
## Im1                 0.953                            
## Im2                 0.931                            
## Im15                0.450                            
## Im10                       0.945                     
## Im14                       0.895                     
## Im12                              0.966              
## Im13                              0.692              
## Im11                              0.498              
## Im17                                     1.005       
## Im18                                     0.786       
## Im6                                             0.994
## Im7                                             0.706
## 
##                  MR4   MR3   MR1   MR2   MR5   MR6   MR7
## SS loadings    2.328 2.062 2.002 1.768 1.707 1.666 1.501
## Proportion Var 0.129 0.115 0.111 0.098 0.095 0.093 0.083
## Cumulative Var 0.129 0.244 0.355 0.453 0.548 0.641 0.724
cat("\nPhi matrix:\n")
## 
## Phi matrix:
print(round(efa_7_obA$Phi, 3))
##       MR4   MR3   MR1   MR2   MR5   MR6   MR7
## MR4 1.000 0.445 0.445 0.394 0.417 0.513 0.319
## MR3 0.445 1.000 0.415 0.283 0.356 0.476 0.260
## MR1 0.445 0.415 1.000 0.295 0.401 0.512 0.197
## MR2 0.394 0.283 0.295 1.000 0.402 0.282 0.489
## MR5 0.417 0.356 0.401 0.402 1.000 0.472 0.206
## MR6 0.513 0.476 0.512 0.282 0.472 1.000 0.303
## MR7 0.319 0.260 0.197 0.489 0.206 0.303 1.000
cat("\nCommunalities:\n")
## 
## Communalities:
print(round(efa_7_obA$communality, 3))
##   Im4   Im3   Im5  Im20  Im22  Im21   Im1   Im2  Im15  Im10  Im14  Im12  Im13 
## 0.966 0.863 0.575 0.787 0.755 0.606 0.927 0.843 0.571 0.889 0.865 0.902 0.669 
##  Im11  Im17  Im18   Im6   Im7 
## 0.384 0.997 0.696 0.954 0.694
# Candidate B (CHOSEN): remove Im16, Im19, Im8, retain Im9
image_data_clean <- image_data %>% select(-Im16, -Im19, -Im8)

efa_7_obB <- fa.sort(fa(image_data_clean, nfactors = 7, rotate = "oblimin",
                        fm = "minres", use = "pairwise"))

cat("=== Candidate B (CHOSEN): 7-Factor Oblimin, -Im16/-Im19/-Im8 (Im9 retained) ===\n")
## === Candidate B (CHOSEN): 7-Factor Oblimin, -Im16/-Im19/-Im8 (Im9 retained) ===
print(efa_7_obB$loadings, cutoff = 0.30)
## 
## Loadings:
##      MR4    MR3    MR5    MR1    MR6    MR7    MR2   
## Im4   1.008                                          
## Im3   0.909                                          
## Im5   0.677                                          
## Im20         0.914                                   
## Im22         0.785                                   
## Im21         0.748                                   
## Im1                 0.953                            
## Im2                 0.930                            
## Im15                0.450                            
## Im12                       0.926                     
## Im13                       0.710                     
## Im11                       0.526                     
## Im17                              0.989              
## Im18                              0.800              
## Im10                                     0.933       
## Im14                                     0.917       
## Im7                                             0.876
## Im6                                             0.873
## Im9                                             0.436
## 
##                  MR4   MR3   MR5   MR1   MR6   MR7   MR2
## SS loadings    2.323 2.045 2.000 1.761 1.682 1.752 1.736
## Proportion Var 0.122 0.108 0.105 0.093 0.089 0.092 0.091
## Cumulative Var 0.122 0.230 0.335 0.428 0.516 0.609 0.700
cat("\nPhi matrix:\n")
## 
## Phi matrix:
print(round(efa_7_obB$Phi, 3))
##       MR4   MR3   MR5   MR1   MR6   MR7   MR2
## MR4 1.000 0.441 0.443 0.414 0.513 0.401 0.339
## MR3 0.441 1.000 0.413 0.357 0.471 0.281 0.294
## MR5 0.443 0.413 1.000 0.404 0.509 0.301 0.223
## MR1 0.414 0.357 0.404 1.000 0.478 0.408 0.255
## MR6 0.513 0.471 0.509 0.478 1.000 0.287 0.331
## MR7 0.401 0.281 0.301 0.408 0.287 1.000 0.549
## MR2 0.339 0.294 0.223 0.255 0.331 0.549 1.000
cat("\nCommunalities:\n")
## 
## Communalities:
print(round(efa_7_obB$communality, 3))
##   Im4   Im3   Im5  Im20  Im22  Im21   Im1   Im2  Im15  Im12  Im13  Im11  Im17 
## 0.968 0.862 0.575 0.787 0.756 0.609 0.927 0.843 0.574 0.848 0.683 0.399 0.980 
##  Im18  Im10  Im14   Im7   Im6   Im9 
## 0.708 0.876 0.876 0.838 0.747 0.459

Chosen solution: Candidate B, 7 factors, Oblimin rotation, Im16, Im19 and Im8 removed, with Im9 retained.

Reasons to keep Im9 for now:

  • Im9 (“French Fashion”) loads exclusively on the French Identity factor with no cross-loadings in the oblimin solution.
  • Its communality is modest but above the minimum threshold of 0.30. Removing it would leave French Identity with only two items, weakening the factor.
  • We prefer not to be overly aggressive with item removal at the EFA stage. Im9 will be re-evaluated in CFA where its fit can be tested more rigorously.

Reasons for Oblimin over Varimax:

The Phi matrix shows inter-factor correlations mostly in the 0.30–0.60 range, confirming that the factors are correlated and oblique rotation is the appropriate choice. Varimax’s orthogonality assumption would distort the solution.

# promax test for completness
# Promax counterpart of Candidate B
efa_7_promax <- fa.sort(fa(image_data_clean, nfactors = 7, rotate = "promax",
                           fm = "minres", use = "pairwise"))

# Quantitative comparison
rot_compare <- data.frame(
  Rotation         = c("Oblimin", "Promax"),
  Cum_Var_Pct      = round(c(efa_7_obB$Vaccounted[3, 7], efa_7_promax$Vaccounted[3, 7]) * 100, 1),
  Mean_Communality = round(c(mean(efa_7_obB$communality), mean(efa_7_promax$communality)), 3),
  Phi_Min_OffDiag  = round(c(min(efa_7_obB$Phi[upper.tri(efa_7_obB$Phi)]),
                             min(efa_7_promax$Phi[upper.tri(efa_7_promax$Phi)])), 3),
  Phi_Max_OffDiag  = round(c(max(efa_7_obB$Phi[upper.tri(efa_7_obB$Phi)]),
                             max(efa_7_promax$Phi[upper.tri(efa_7_promax$Phi)])), 3)
)

rot_compare %>%
  kbl(caption = "Oblimin vs Promax — same item set, same factor count",
      col.names = c("Rotation","Cum. Var. %","Mean h²",
                    "Min |Phi| off-diag","Max |Phi| off-diag")) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
  row_spec(1, bold = TRUE, background = "#d5f5e3")
Oblimin vs Promax — same item set, same factor count
Rotation Cum. Var. % Mean h² Min |Phi| off-diag Max |Phi| off-diag
Oblimin 75.3 0.753 0.223 0.549
Promax 75.3 0.753 0.314 0.599 
cat("\n=== Promax loadings (for comparison) ===\n")
## 
## === Promax loadings (for comparison) ===
print(efa_7_promax$loadings, cutoff = 0.30)
## 
## Loadings:
##      MR4    MR5    MR3    MR1    MR2    MR6    MR7   
## Im4   1.046                                          
## Im3   0.941                                          
## Im5   0.699                                          
## Im1          1.020                                   
## Im2          1.000                                   
## Im15         0.465                                   
## Im20                0.952                            
## Im22                0.819                            
## Im21                0.780                            
## Im12                       1.055                     
## Im13                       0.800                     
## Im11                       0.601                     
## Im7                               0.938              
## Im6                               0.935              
## Im9                               0.445              
## Im17                                     1.058       
## Im18                                     0.853       
## Im10                                            0.930
## Im14                                            0.915
## 
##                  MR4   MR5   MR3   MR1   MR2   MR6   MR7
## SS loadings    2.490 2.284 2.215 2.260 1.976 1.912 1.738
## Proportion Var 0.131 0.120 0.117 0.119 0.104 0.101 0.091
## Cumulative Var 0.131 0.251 0.368 0.487 0.591 0.691 0.783
cat("\n=== Promax Phi matrix ===\n")
## 
## === Promax Phi matrix ===
print(round(efa_7_promax$Phi, 3))
##       MR4   MR5   MR3   MR1   MR2   MR6   MR7
## MR4 1.000 0.524 0.500 0.516 0.412 0.571 0.420
## MR5 0.524 1.000 0.502 0.542 0.347 0.588 0.337
## MR3 0.500 0.502 1.000 0.483 0.384 0.530 0.323
## MR1 0.516 0.542 0.483 1.000 0.452 0.599 0.498
## MR2 0.412 0.347 0.384 0.452 1.000 0.469 0.575
## MR6 0.571 0.588 0.530 0.599 0.469 1.000 0.314
## MR7 0.420 0.337 0.323 0.498 0.575 0.314 1.000

Difference between Promax vs Oblimin: Both rotations achieve clean simple structure and identical item-to-factor assignment. Promax, however, inflates the inter-factor correlations through its k = 4 power transform. Oblimin’s correlation range (typically 0.22–0.55) is a more realistic representation of how brand-image dimensions actually relate to each other in retail data. Since loadings are essentially identical, the more conservative rotation is the better default.

1.5 Final EFA Solution (7 Factors, Oblimin)

The final solution chosen for EFA is presented in this section, with loading matrix, factor intercorrelations and communalities (Candidate B: Im16, Im19, Im8 removed; Im9 retained).

# print the loadings
efa_final <- efa_7_obB

# Full loading matrix
print(efa_final$loadings, cutoff = 0.30)
## 
## Loadings:
##      MR4    MR3    MR5    MR1    MR6    MR7    MR2   
## Im4   1.008                                          
## Im3   0.909                                          
## Im5   0.677                                          
## Im20         0.914                                   
## Im22         0.785                                   
## Im21         0.748                                   
## Im1                 0.953                            
## Im2                 0.930                            
## Im15                0.450                            
## Im12                       0.926                     
## Im13                       0.710                     
## Im11                       0.526                     
## Im17                              0.989              
## Im18                              0.800              
## Im10                                     0.933       
## Im14                                     0.917       
## Im7                                             0.876
## Im6                                             0.873
## Im9                                             0.436
## 
##                  MR4   MR3   MR5   MR1   MR6   MR7   MR2
## SS loadings    2.323 2.045 2.000 1.761 1.682 1.752 1.736
## Proportion Var 0.122 0.108 0.105 0.093 0.089 0.092 0.091
## Cumulative Var 0.122 0.230 0.335 0.428 0.516 0.609 0.700
# print phi matrix, and already assign tentative names for the factors
phi_mat <- round(efa_final$Phi, 3)

phi_labels <- c("Decoration","Assortment","Atmosphere",
                "Luxury","FrenchId","Trendy","Gourmet")
rownames(phi_mat) <- phi_labels
colnames(phi_mat) <- phi_labels

phi_df <- as.data.frame(phi_mat) %>%
  rownames_to_column("Factor_Row") %>%
  pivot_longer(-Factor_Row, names_to = "Factor_Col", values_to = "r") %>%
  mutate(
    Factor_Row = factor(Factor_Row, levels = phi_labels),
    Factor_Col = factor(Factor_Col, levels = rev(phi_labels)),
    label      = ifelse(Factor_Row == Factor_Col, "1.00", sprintf("%.2f", r)),
    is_diag    = Factor_Row == Factor_Col
  )

ggplot(phi_df, aes(x = Factor_Row, y = Factor_Col, fill = r)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_text(aes(label = label,
                fontface = ifelse(is_diag, "plain", "bold"),
                color    = ifelse(abs(r) > 0.45 & !is_diag, "white", "grey20")),
            size = 4.0) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low      = "#2980b9",
    mid      = "#f0f4f8",
    high     = "#c0392b",
    midpoint = 0,
    limits   = c(-1, 1),
    name     = "Correlation"
  ) +
  scale_color_identity() +
  scale_x_discrete(position = "top") +
  coord_fixed(clip="off") +
  labs(
    title    = "Factor Intercorrelation Matrix (Phi) — Oblimin EFA",
    subtitle = "Values show pairwise correlations between latent factors",
    x = NULL, y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title      = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
    plot.subtitle   = element_text(color = "grey40", size = 10, margin=margin(b=22)),
    axis.text.x.top      = element_text(face = "bold", angle = 30, hjust = 0, size = 10),
    axis.text.y     = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
    panel.grid      = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "right"
  )

Factor correlations range from 0.22 to 0.55. Values consistently above 0.30 confirm that oblique rotation was the correct choice. Orthogonal Varimax would have forced artificial independence between correlated constructs and distorted the solution.

#heatmap of the loadings
factor_names <- c("MV1"="Luxury","MV2"="Gourmet","MV3"="Atmosphere",
                  "MV4"="Decoration","MV5"="Assortment","MV6"="Trendy","MV7"="FrenchId")

# Rename columns of loadings matrix
ld_raw <- as.data.frame(unclass(efa_final$loadings))
# Try to rename based on actual column names
actual_cols <- colnames(ld_raw)
factor_labels <- paste0("F", 1:7)
names(factor_labels) <- actual_cols

ld_df <- ld_raw %>%
  rownames_to_column("Item") %>%
  pivot_longer(-Item, names_to = "Factor", values_to = "Loading") %>%
  mutate(Factor = recode(Factor, !!!setNames(factor_labels, actual_cols)))

item_order <- ld_df %>%
  group_by(Item) %>%
  slice_max(abs(Loading), n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  arrange(Factor, desc(abs(Loading))) %>%
  pull(Item)

ld_df %>%
  mutate(Item = factor(Item, levels = rev(item_order))) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Factor, y = Item, fill = Loading)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(abs(Loading) >= 0.30, sprintf("%.2f", Loading), "")),
            size = 2.9, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "#c0392b", mid = "white", high = "#2980b9",
                       midpoint = 0, limits = c(-1, 1), name = "Loading") +
  labs(title    = "Oblimin Factor Loadings - Final 7-Factor EFA",
       subtitle = "Only loadings |>= 0.30| labelled | Removed: Im8, Im16, Im19",
       x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_text(face = "bold", angle = 20, hjust = 1),
        plot.title  = element_text(face = "bold"))

# communality plot
data.frame(Item = names(efa_final$communality),
           h2   = as.numeric(efa_final$communality)) %>%
  mutate(Quality = case_when(h2 >= 0.50 ~ "Good",
                             h2 >= 0.40 ~ "Acceptable",
                             TRUE       ~ "Weak")) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(Item, h2), y = h2, fill = Quality)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.75) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.50, linetype="dashed", color="#c0392b", linewidth=0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.40, linetype="dotted", color="#e67e22", linewidth=0.7) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Good"="#27ae60","Acceptable"="#f39c12","Weak"="#e74c3c")) +
  labs(title = "Item Communalities - Final EFA", x = NULL, y = "h2") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"), legend.position = "top")

After EFA, we can identify those 7 factors:

Seven dimensions of Galeries Lafayette brand image:

  1. Decoration (Im3, Im4, Im5) : artistic and creative visual merchandising
  2. Assortment (Im1, Im2, Im15) : breadth and professional curation of range
  3. Atmosphere (Im20, Im21, Im22) ; relaxing, intimate shopping environment
  4. Luxury (Im11, Im12, Im13) : luxury brands and high-quality cosmetics
  5. French Identity (Im6, Im7) : embodiment of France and savoir-vivre
  6. Trendy (Im17, Im18) : trendy, hip positioning
  7. Gourmet (Im10, Im14) : gourmet food and specialities

All seven factors have clear marketing interpretations and zero cross-loading items.

Concerning Im11 (Luxury factor): Im11 shows a communality below the 0.50 threshold, meaning the seven-factor solution explains a relatively limited share of its variance. However, we decided to not drop it for now, because it loads cleanly on the Luxury factor with no cross-loadings and removing it would leave Luxury with only two items (Im12 and Im13). Therefore we decide to retain Im11 in the EFA solution and re-evaluate it in CFA.

2. Confirmatory Factor Analysis (CFA)

2.1 Comparison of different CFA solutions.

After EFA, we continue with confirmatory factor analysis (CFA). Here, three CFA models are estimated in sequence. After each model, the modification indices are inspected to identify theoretically justified improvements, providing explicit justification for every change made. The MLR estimator was used for robust standard errors and fit statistics in the presence of potential non-normality, and for missingness we chose to apply “fiml”.

# first model
cfa_m1 <- '
  Image_Decoration     =~ Im4 + Im3 + Im5
  Image_Assortment     =~ Im1 + Im2 + Im15
  Image_Atmosphere     =~ Im20 + Im22 + Im21
  Image_Luxury         =~ Im12 + Im13 + Im11
  Image_FrenchIdentity =~ Im7 + Im6 + Im9
  Image_Trendy         =~ Im17 + Im18
  Image_Gourmet        =~ Im10 + Im14
'
fit1 <- cfa(cfa_m1, data = data_raw, estimator = "MLR", missing = "fiml")

fm1 <- fitMeasures(fit1, c("chisq","df","cfi","tli","rmsea","srmr"))
data.frame(Index = names(fm1), Value = round(as.numeric(fm1), 3)) %>%
  kbl(caption = "Model 1 (Initial) — Fit Indices", col.names = c("Index","Value")) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"), full_width = FALSE, position = "left")
Model 1 (Initial) — Fit Indices
Index Value
chisq 474.908
df 131.000
cfi 0.953
tli 0.939
rmsea 0.069
srmr 0.069 
# mod. index of the first model
modindices(fit1, sort. = TRUE) %>%
  filter(mi > 10) %>%
  select(lhs, op, rhs, mi, epc, sepc.all) %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), round, 3)) %>%
  kbl(caption = "Modification indices > 10 (Model 1 — Initial CFA)") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"), full_width = FALSE)
Modification indices > 10 (Model 1 — Initial CFA)
lhs op rhs mi epc sepc.all
Im1 Im2 99.399 0.907 4.565
Image_Luxury =~ Im9 74.260 0.497 0.364
Image_Luxury =~ Im15 72.252 0.421 0.348
Image_Trendy =~ Im9 58.746 0.355 0.315
Image_Trendy =~ Im15 55.180 0.302 0.301
Image_Decoration =~ Im15 48.008 0.238 0.259
Image_Atmosphere =~ Im15 38.347 0.232 0.244
Image_FrenchIdentity =~ Im15 32.278 0.217 0.201
Image_Assortment =~ Im9 26.810 0.215 0.203
Im7 Im6 25.945 0.469 1.490
Image_Gourmet =~ Im15 24.775 0.260 0.175
Image_Trendy =~ Im13 23.439 0.221 0.220
Im13 Im11 21.644 -0.193 -0.342
Image_Atmosphere =~ Im9 20.687 0.205 0.192
Image_Decoration =~ Im9 19.329 0.184 0.178
Image_Trendy =~ Im7 17.688 -0.149 -0.148
Image_Trendy =~ Im12 17.109 -0.181 -0.190
Image_Luxury =~ Im2 16.999 -0.158 -0.122
Im22 Im21 15.711 -0.292 -0.466
Im1 Im15 15.324 -0.153 -0.462
Image_Assortment =~ Im20 14.429 -0.156 -0.133
Image_Assortment =~ Im13 13.486 0.139 0.147
Im12 Im11 13.435 0.146 0.289
Image_Gourmet =~ Im11 12.549 0.214 0.151
Im15 Im9 12.408 0.154 0.162
Im4 Im3 12.336 0.280 1.836
Im20 Im21 11.942 0.234 0.343
Image_Luxury =~ Im6 11.308 -0.141 -0.116
Image_Atmosphere =~ Im12 10.958 -0.115 -0.127
Image_Assortment =~ Im12 10.916 -0.119 -0.134
Image_Gourmet =~ Im6 10.546 -0.232 -0.156

Fit is reasonable but can be improved. The largest modification index suggests freeing the residual covariance between Im1 and Im2 (Large Assortment and Assortment Variety). This is theoretically justified: both items refer to the breadth of the product range with slightly different wording, so some overlap in their unique variances beyond the shared Assortment factor is expected. So we allow them to correlate with each other in the next model (e.g. no directional effect).

# the second model with Im1 and Im2 having correlation between them
cfa_m2 <- '
  Image_Decoration     =~ Im4 + Im3 + Im5
  Image_Assortment     =~ Im1 + Im2 + Im15
  Image_Atmosphere     =~ Im20 + Im22 + Im21
  Image_Luxury         =~ Im12 + Im13 + Im11
  Image_FrenchIdentity =~ Im7 + Im6 + Im9
  Image_Trendy         =~ Im17 + Im18
  Image_Gourmet        =~ Im10 + Im14
  Im1 ~~ Im2
'
fit2 <- cfa(cfa_m2, data = data_raw, estimator = "MLR", missing = "fiml")

fm2 <- fitMeasures(fit2, c("chisq","df","cfi","tli","rmsea","srmr"))
data.frame(Index = names(fm2), Value = round(as.numeric(fm2), 3)) %>%
  kbl(caption = "Model 2 (+Im1~~Im2) — Fit Indices", col.names = c("Index","Value")) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"), full_width = FALSE, position = "left")
Model 2 (+Im1Im2) — Fit Indices
Index Value
chisq 365.696
df 130.000
cfi 0.968
tli 0.958
rmsea 0.057
srmr 0.054 
# mod index of second model
modindices(fit2, sort. = TRUE) %>%
  filter(mi > 10) %>%
  select(lhs, op, rhs, mi, epc, sepc.all) %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), round, 3)) %>%
  kbl(caption = "Modification indices > 10 (Model 2 — after Im1~~Im2)") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"), full_width = FALSE)
Modification indices > 10 (Model 2 — after Im1Im2)
lhs op rhs mi epc sepc.all
Image_Luxury =~ Im9 74.664 0.498 0.365
Image_Trendy =~ Im9 58.662 0.350 0.313
Image_Assortment =~ Im9 52.765 0.430 0.318
Im7 Im6 26.622 0.476 1.516
Image_Trendy =~ Im13 22.559 0.212 0.212
Image_Atmosphere =~ Im9 20.898 0.206 0.193
Im13 Im11 20.272 -0.181 -0.323
Image_Decoration =~ Im9 19.482 0.185 0.179
Image_Trendy =~ Im7 17.928 -0.149 -0.149
Image_Assortment =~ Im13 16.882 0.288 0.239
Image_Trendy =~ Im12 16.488 -0.173 -0.183
Im22 Im21 14.840 -0.280 -0.447
Im12 Im11 14.608 0.147 0.289
Image_Assortment =~ Im12 14.383 -0.254 -0.224
Image_Gourmet =~ Im11 12.813 0.216 0.153
Im20 Im21 11.749 0.229 0.336
Image_Luxury =~ Im6 11.189 -0.140 -0.115
Image_Atmosphere =~ Im12 10.496 -0.112 -0.124
Image_Gourmet =~ Im6 10.455 -0.231 -0.156
Im4 Im3 10.292 0.251 1.637

Fit improves but RMSEA and TLI remain slightly below ideal thresholds. The modification indices now highlight Im9 cross-loading onto multiple factors. This was already an item that was flagged in the EFA. Given this evidence from both EFA and the CFA modification indices, we remove Im9. French Identity remains defined by Im7 (“French Savoir-vivre”) and Im6 (“France”), both of which have strong loadings.

# 3rd model without Im9
cfa_m3_with_Im11 <- '
  Image_Decoration     =~ Im4 + Im3 + Im5
  Image_Assortment     =~ Im1 + Im2 + Im15
  Image_Atmosphere     =~ Im20 + Im22 + Im21
  Image_Luxury         =~ Im12 + Im13 + Im11
  Image_FrenchIdentity =~ Im7 + Im6
  Image_Trendy         =~ Im17 + Im18
  Image_Gourmet        =~ Im10 + Im14
  Im1 ~~ Im2
'
fit_m3_with_Im11 <- cfa(cfa_m3_with_Im11, data = data_raw,
                        estimator = "MLR", missing = "fiml")

fm3 <- fitMeasures(fit_m3_with_Im11, c("chisq","df","cfi","tli","rmsea","srmr"))
data.frame(Index = names(fm3), Value = round(as.numeric(fm3), 3)) %>%
  kbl(caption = "Model 3 (-Im9, with Im11) - Fit Indices", col.names = c("Index","Value")) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"), full_width = FALSE, position = "left")
Model 3 (-Im9, with Im11) - Fit Indices
Index Value
chisq 242.775
df 113.000
cfi 0.982
tli 0.975
rmsea 0.046
srmr 0.030 
# mod index of model 3
modindices(fit_m3_with_Im11, sort. = TRUE) %>%
  filter(mi > 10) %>%
  select(lhs, op, rhs, mi, epc, sepc.all) %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), round, 3)) %>%
  kbl(caption = "Modification indices > 10 (Model 3 — with Im11)") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"), full_width = FALSE)
Modification indices > 10 (Model 3 — with Im11)
lhs op rhs mi epc sepc.all
Image_Trendy =~ Im13 22.434 0.211 0.211
Im13 Im11 20.373 -0.181 -0.324
Image_Assortment =~ Im13 16.749 0.287 0.238
Image_Trendy =~ Im12 16.361 -0.172 -0.182
Im22 Im21 14.807 -0.279 -0.446
Im12 Im11 14.683 0.147 0.290
Image_Assortment =~ Im12 14.257 -0.253 -0.223
Image_Gourmet =~ Im11 12.767 0.216 0.153
Im20 Im21 11.552 0.227 0.333
Im4 Im3 10.440 0.253 1.649
Image_Atmosphere =~ Im12 10.409 -0.111 -0.123

No remaining modification index provides both a large MI value and a theoretically meaningful justification. But we still want to test the item Im11 which was flagged at the end of Section 1, below.

2.1.1 Item 11 Retention test (Model 3 vs Model 4)

# model 4 without item 11
cfa_m4_no_Im11 <- '
  Image_Decoration     =~ Im4 + Im3 + Im5
  Image_Assortment     =~ Im1 + Im2 + Im15
  Image_Atmosphere     =~ Im20 + Im22 + Im21
  Image_Luxury         =~ Im12 + Im13
  Image_FrenchIdentity =~ Im7 + Im6
  Image_Trendy         =~ Im17 + Im18
  Image_Gourmet        =~ Im10 + Im14
  Im1 ~~ Im2
'
fit_m4_no_Im11 <- cfa(cfa_m4_no_Im11, data = data_raw,
                      estimator = "MLR", missing = "fiml")
# fit measurement comparison
calc_cr_ave <- function(fit_obj, factor_name) {
  ld <- standardizedSolution(fit_obj) %>%
    filter(op == "=~", lhs == factor_name) %>%
    pull(est.std)
  cr  <- sum(ld)^2 / (sum(ld)^2 + sum(1 - ld^2))
  ave <- mean(ld^2)
  c(N_items = length(ld), CR = round(cr, 3), AVE = round(ave, 3))
}

im11_fit_compare <- rbind(
  "Model 3 - with Im11" = fitMeasures(fit_m3_with_Im11,
                                      c("chisq","df","cfi","tli","rmsea","srmr","aic")),
  "Model 4 - no Im11"   = fitMeasures(fit_m4_no_Im11,
                                      c("chisq","df","cfi","tli","rmsea","srmr","aic"))
) %>% round(3) %>% as.data.frame()

im11_fit_compare$Luxury_CR  <- c(calc_cr_ave(fit_m3_with_Im11, "Image_Luxury")["CR"],
                                 calc_cr_ave(fit_m4_no_Im11,   "Image_Luxury")["CR"])
im11_fit_compare$Luxury_AVE <- c(calc_cr_ave(fit_m3_with_Im11, "Image_Luxury")["AVE"],
                                 calc_cr_ave(fit_m4_no_Im11,   "Image_Luxury")["AVE"])

im11_fit_compare %>%
  kbl(caption = "Im11 retention test - fit & convergent validity",
      col.names = c("chi2","df","CFI","TLI","RMSEA","SRMR","AIC","Luxury CR","Luxury AVE")) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
  row_spec(2, bold = TRUE, background = "#d5f5e3")
Im11 retention test - fit & convergent validity
chi2 df CFI TLI RMSEA SRMR AIC Luxury CR Luxury AVE
Model 3 - with Im11 242.775 113 0.982 0.975 0.046 0.030 24695.30 0.829 0.623
Model 4 - no Im11 186.007 97 0.987 0.982 0.041 0.025 23211.71 0.859 0.753 
# modification index of model 4
modindices(fit_m4_no_Im11, sort. = TRUE) %>%
  filter(mi > 10) %>%
  select(lhs, op, rhs, mi, epc, sepc.all) %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), round, 3)) %>%
  kbl(caption = "Modification indices > 10 (Model 4 — Final CFA without Im11)") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"), full_width = FALSE)
Modification indices > 10 (Model 4 — Final CFA without Im11)
lhs op rhs mi epc sepc.all
Im22 Im21 15.487 -0.286 -0.458
Im20 Im21 12.057 0.232 0.340
Im4 Im3 10.339 0.252 1.643

Decision: drop Im11. All fit indices improve when Im11 is removed. The most striking change is in Luxury AVE, which goes from 0.62 to 0.75 (+ 21 %), with CR also improving from 0.83 to 0.86. The standardised loading of Im11 itself is only ≈ 0.61, which is below the 0.70 cut-off. “High-quality cosmetics” is conceptually closer to a quality/assortment dimension than to a luxury-brand dimension, which could be reason why its loading was borderline.

# Final CFA model = Model 4 (no Im11). 
cfa_final <- cfa_m4_no_Im11
fit_cfa   <- fit_m4_no_Im11
fit3      <- fit_m3_with_Im11   # kept for backward-compat with the fit-comparison table below

2.2 Fit Comparison

The table below compares fit indices across the four CFA models. Acceptable thresholds are CFI > 0.95, TLI > 0.95, RMSEA < 0.05.

# comparison table for all fit measurement
get_fit <- function(fit_obj) {
  fm <- fitMeasures(fit_obj, c("chisq","df","cfi","tli","rmsea",
                               "rmsea.ci.lower","rmsea.ci.upper","srmr"))
  c(chi2df   = round(unname(fm["chisq"]) / unname(fm["df"]), 3),
    cfi      = round(unname(fm["cfi"]),             3),
    tli      = round(unname(fm["tli"]),             3),
    rmsea    = round(unname(fm["rmsea"]),            3),
    rmsea_lo = round(unname(fm["rmsea.ci.lower"]),   3),
    rmsea_hi = round(unname(fm["rmsea.ci.upper"]),   3),
    srmr     = round(unname(fm["srmr"]),             3))
}

rbind("Model 1 - Initial"           = get_fit(fit1),
      "Model 2 - +Im1~~Im2"         = get_fit(fit2),
      "Model 3 - -Im9 (with Im11)"  = get_fit(fit3),
      "Model 4 - Final (-Im11)"     = get_fit(fit_cfa)) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  setNames(c("Chi2/df","CFI","TLI","RMSEA","RMSEA lo","RMSEA hi","SRMR")) %>%
  kbl(caption = "CFA model fit progression") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
  row_spec(4, bold = TRUE, background = "#d5f5e3")
CFA model fit progression
Chi2/df CFI TLI RMSEA RMSEA lo RMSEA hi SRMR
Model 1 - Initial 3.625 0.953 0.939 0.069 0.062 0.076 0.069
Model 2 - +Im1Im2 2.813 0.968 0.958 0.057 0.050 0.064 0.054
Model 3 - -Im9 (with Im11) 2.148 0.982 0.975 0.046 0.038 0.053 0.030
Model 4 - Final (-Im11) 1.918 0.987 0.982 0.041 0.032 0.050 0.025

The final CFA model (Model 4: 7 factors, Im11 removed) achieves CFI ≈ 0.983, TLI ≈ 0.976, RMSEA ≈ 0.046 and SRMR ≈ 0.030. We continue forward with this model.

# get the full summary of the model
summary(fit_cfa, fit.measures = TRUE, standardized = TRUE, rsquare = TRUE)
## lavaan 0.6-21 ended normally after 94 iterations
## 
##   Estimator                                         ML
##   Optimization method                           NLMINB

##

##                    Estimate
##     Im4               0.939
##     Im3               0.878
##     Im5               0.580
##     Im1               0.567
