##     Im10              0.841
##     Im14              0.919
# draw the latent factors, and the items which are loading on temp
cfa_node_labels <- c(
  "Im4","Im3","Im5",
  "Im1","Im2","Im15",
  "Im20","Im22","Im21",
  "Im12","Im13",
  "Im7","Im6",
  "Im17","Im18",
  "Im10","Im14",
  "Decortn","Assrtmt","Atmosph","Luxury","FrenchId","Trendy","Gourmet"
)

semPaths(fit_cfa, what="std", whatLabels="std", layout="tree2", rotation=2,
         style="lisrel", residuals=FALSE, intercepts=FALSE,
         nCharNodes=0, nodeLabels=cfa_node_labels,
         sizeLat=9, sizeMan=5, edge.label.cex=0.9,
         label.cex=1.0, curvePivot=FALSE, cardinal=FALSE,
         exoCov=FALSE, weighted=FALSE,
         mar=c(1, 6, 2, 1),
         main="CFA Final Model - Standardised Loadings")

CFA Final Model

Each circle represents a latent factor and each rectangle an observed survey item. The number on each arrow is the standardised loading.

2.3 Standardised Loadings and Reliability

The table below presents the standardised factor loadings, standard errors, and item-level R² for the final CFA model, followed by Composite Reliability (CR ≥ 0.70) and Average Variance Extracted (AVE ≥ 0.50 for convergent validity).

#standardized loadings
std_ld <- standardizedSolution(fit_cfa) %>%
  filter(op == "=~") %>%
  transmute(Factor = lhs, Item = rhs,
            Std_Loading = round(est.std, 3),
            SE = round(se, 3), z = round(z, 2),
            p  = ifelse(pvalue < .001, "< .001", as.character(round(pvalue,3))),
            Item_R2 = round(est.std^2, 3))

std_ld %>%
  kbl(caption = "Standardised CFA loadings - Final model",
      col.names = c("Factor","Item","Std. Loading","SE","z","p","Item R2")) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover","condensed"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
  column_spec(3, bold = TRUE,
              color = ifelse(std_ld$Std_Loading >= 0.70, "#27ae60", "#e67e22"))
Standardised CFA loadings - Final model
Factor Item Std. Loading SE z p Item R2
Image_Decoration Im4 0.969 0.009 106.50 < .001 0.939
Image_Decoration Im3 0.937 0.013 70.88 < .001 0.878
Image_Decoration Im5 0.761 0.025 31.00 < .001 0.580
Image_Assortment Im1 0.753 0.026 28.58 < .001 0.567
Image_Assortment Im2 0.704 0.031 22.56 < .001 0.496
Image_Assortment Im15 0.868 0.029 29.95 < .001 0.753
Image_Atmosphere Im20 0.845 0.021 40.32 < .001 0.714
Image_Atmosphere Im22 0.877 0.021 41.30 < .001 0.770
Image_Atmosphere Im21 0.783 0.033 23.68 < .001 0.613
Image_Luxury Im12 0.818 0.028 29.10 < .001 0.669
Image_Luxury Im13 0.915 0.021 43.65 < .001 0.838
Image_FrenchIdentity Im7 0.956 0.027 35.80 < .001 0.915
Image_FrenchIdentity Im6 0.812 0.029 27.86 < .001 0.660
Image_Trendy Im17 0.972 0.016 58.98 < .001 0.945
Image_Trendy Im18 0.854 0.026 33.29 < .001 0.728
Image_Gourmet Im10 0.917 0.021 42.82 < .001 0.841
Image_Gourmet Im14 0.959 0.016 61.31 < .001 0.919 
# AVE and CR 
sl <- standardizedSolution(fit_cfa) %>%
  filter(op == "=~") %>% select(Factor = lhs, lambda = est.std)

cr_ave <- sl %>% group_by(Factor) %>%
  summarise(N = n(),
            CR  = round(sum(lambda)^2 / (sum(lambda)^2 + sum(1-lambda^2)), 3),
            AVE = round(mean(lambda^2), 3), .groups="drop") %>%
  mutate(CR_ok  = ifelse(CR  >= .70, "Yes", "No"),
         AVE_ok = ifelse(AVE >= .50, "Yes", "No"))

cr_ave %>%
  kbl(caption = "Composite Reliability and AVE",
      col.names = c("Factor","N Items","CR","AVE","CR >= .70","AVE >= .50")) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
  column_spec(3, bold=TRUE, color=ifelse(cr_ave$CR  >= .70, "#27ae60","#c0392b")) %>%
  column_spec(4, bold=TRUE, color=ifelse(cr_ave$AVE >= .50, "#27ae60","#e67e22"))
Composite Reliability and AVE
Factor N Items CR AVE CR >= .70 AVE >= .50
Image_Assortment 3 0.820 0.605 Yes Yes
Image_Atmosphere 3 0.874 0.699 Yes Yes
Image_Decoration 3 0.922 0.799 Yes Yes
Image_FrenchIdentity 2 0.880 0.787 Yes Yes
Image_Gourmet 2 0.936 0.880 Yes Yes
Image_Luxury 2 0.859 0.753 Yes Yes
Image_Trendy 2 0.911 0.837 Yes Yes

2.4 Cronbach’s Alpha

Cronbach’s alpha is computed for each factor using its final CFA items. Values of alpha ≥ 0.70 indicate acceptable internal consistency.

library(psych)

# Define final CFA item groups
cfa_item_groups <- list(
  Image_Decoration     = c("Im4", "Im3", "Im5"),
  Image_Assortment     = c("Im1", "Im2", "Im15"),
  Image_Atmosphere     = c("Im20", "Im22", "Im21"),
  Image_Luxury         = c("Im12", "Im13"),
  Image_FrenchIdentity = c("Im7", "Im6"),
  Image_Trendy         = c("Im17", "Im18"),
  Image_Gourmet        = c("Im10", "Im14")
)

cronbach_results <- lapply(names(cfa_item_groups), function(factor_name) {
  items <- cfa_item_groups[[factor_name]]
  alpha_val <- psych::alpha(data_raw[, items], na.rm = TRUE)$total$raw_alpha
  data.frame(
    Factor     = factor_name,
    N_Items    = length(items),
    Alpha      = round(alpha_val, 3),
    Acceptable = ifelse(alpha_val >= 0.70, "Yes", "No"),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}) %>% bind_rows()

cronbach_results %>%
  kbl(caption = "Cronbach's Alpha for each factor (final CFA items)",
      col.names = c("Factor", "N Items", "Alpha", "Alpha >= .70")) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
  column_spec(3, bold = TRUE,
              color = ifelse(cronbach_results$Alpha >= 0.70, "#27ae60", "#c0392b"))
Cronbach’s Alpha for each factor (final CFA items)
Factor N Items Alpha Alpha >= .70
Image_Decoration 3 0.916 Yes
Image_Assortment 3 0.886 Yes
Image_Atmosphere 3 0.876 Yes
Image_Luxury 2 0.853 Yes
Image_FrenchIdentity 2 0.879 Yes
Image_Trendy 2 0.906 Yes
Image_Gourmet 2 0.923 Yes

All factors with three or more items show strong alpha values (≥ 0.70). The two-item factors (Trendy, FrenchIdentity, Gourmet) tend to have slightly lower alpha, which is expected since Cronbach’s alpha is sensitive to the number of items. For these factors, the Composite Reliability values from previous section 2.3 provide more appropriate reliability estimates.

2.5 Variance Explained per Factor

The bar chart below shows the SS loadings for each image dimension, derived from the EFA solution that matches the final CFA structure (Im16, Im19, Im8, Im9 and Im11 removed). SS loadings reflect how strongly and broadly each factor is defined by its items. A higher value means the factor captures a larger share of common variance.

# Re-run EFA on the exact item set used in the final CFA.
image_data_cfa <- image_data %>% select(-Im16, -Im19, -Im8, -Im9, -Im11)
efa_cfa_match <- fa.sort(fa(image_data_cfa, nfactors = 7, rotate = "oblimin",
                             fm = "minres", use = "pairwise"))

efa_vaccounted <- efa_cfa_match$Vaccounted
actual_factor_names <- colnames(efa_vaccounted)

# Map EFA factor codes to interpretable names based on loading inspection
# (factors are ordered by SS loadings, so we assign names after running)
factor_labels_map <- setNames(
  c("Decoration", "Assortment", "Atmosphere", "Luxury",
    "FrenchIdentity", "Trendy", "Gourmet"),
  actual_factor_names[1:7]
)

variance_df <- data.frame(
  Factor  = factor_labels_map[actual_factor_names],
  SS_Load = round(efa_vaccounted["SS loadings", ], 3),
  Var_Pct = round(efa_vaccounted["Proportion Var", ] * 100, 2),
  Cum_Pct = round(cumsum(efa_vaccounted["Proportion Var", ]) * 100, 2)
) %>% na.omit()

variance_df %>%
  kbl(caption = "SS Loadings and Variance Explained — Final 7-Factor EFA (Oblimin, matching CFA items)",
      col.names = c("Factor", "SS Loadings", "Variance (%)", "Cumulative (%)")) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE)
SS Loadings and Variance Explained — Final 7-Factor EFA (Oblimin, matching CFA items)
Factor SS Loadings Variance (%) Cumulative (%)
MR4 Decoration 2.445 14.38 14.38
MR3 Assortment 2.151 12.66 27.04
MR1 Atmosphere 2.124 12.50 39.53
MR2 Luxury 1.817 10.69 50.22
MR7 FrenchIdentity 1.763 10.37 60.59
MR5 Trendy 1.604 9.43 70.02
MR6 Gourmet 1.580 9.29 79.32 
dim_colours <- c("#4db6ac", "#ffd54f", "#ff8a65", "#90a4ae",
                 "#ce93d8", "#90caf9", "#a5d6a7")

ggplot(variance_df,
       aes(x = reorder(Factor, -SS_Load), y = SS_Load, fill = Factor)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.72, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(SS_Load, 2), "\n(", Var_Pct, "%)")),
            vjust = -0.3, size = 3.2, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = setNames(dim_colours, variance_df$Factor)) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.20))) +
  labs(title    = "SS Loadings by Image Dimension (Oblimin, 7 Factors, CFA item set)",
       subtitle = "Numbers show SS loadings and % variance explained",
       x = "Image Dimension", y = "SS Loadings") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_blank())

3. Path Modelling - Full Mediation

3.1 Full Mediation Model

Now that we have confirmed our latent factor structure, we will do path modeling in the next section. Here, the full causal model extends the measurement model with structural paths routing all seven image dimensions through two mediators (Satisfaction and Affective Commitment) to two outcome variables (Repurchase Intention and Co-Creation Intention). At first, we do not define the direct paths from image dimensions to outcomes are included. We here have at first only the full mediation specification.

# define a  full mediation model  and fit it
sem_model <- '
  Image_Decoration     =~ Im4  + Im3  + Im5
  Image_Assortment     =~ Im1  + Im2  + Im15
  Image_Atmosphere     =~ Im22 + Im20 + Im21
  Image_Luxury         =~ Im12 + Im13
  Image_FrenchIdentity =~ Im7  + Im6
  Image_Gourmet        =~ Im14 + Im10
  Image_Trendy         =~ Im17 + Im18
  Im1 ~~ Im2

  Satisfaction        =~ SAT_1  + SAT_2  + SAT_3
  AffectiveCommitment =~ COM_A1 + COM_A2 + COM_A3 + COM_A4
  Repurchase          =~ C_REP1 + C_REP2 + C_REP3
  CoCreation          =~ C_CR1  + C_CR3  + C_CR4

  Satisfaction        ~ Image_Decoration + Image_Assortment + Image_Atmosphere +
                        Image_Luxury + Image_FrenchIdentity + Image_Gourmet + Image_Trendy
  AffectiveCommitment ~ Image_Decoration + Image_Assortment + Image_Atmosphere +
                        Image_Luxury + Image_FrenchIdentity + Image_Gourmet + Image_Trendy
  Repurchase  ~ Satisfaction + AffectiveCommitment
  CoCreation  ~ Satisfaction + AffectiveCommitment
'
fit_sem <- cfa(sem_model, data = data_raw, estimator = "MLR", missing = "fiml")

3.2 Model Fit

The full causal model is evaluated against the same fit thresholds as the CFA.

# get the fit measurements
fm_sem <- fitMeasures(fit_sem,
  c("chisq","df","pvalue","cfi","tli","rmsea","rmsea.ci.lower",
    "rmsea.ci.upper","srmr","aic","bic"))

data.frame(Index = names(fm_sem), Value = round(as.numeric(fm_sem), 3)) %>%
  kbl(caption = "Full mediation SEM - fit indices", col.names = c("Index","Value")) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"), full_width = FALSE, position = "left")
Full mediation SEM - fit indices
Index Value
chisq 616.238
df 364.000
pvalue 0.000
cfi 0.977
tli 0.973
rmsea 0.035
rmsea.ci.lower 0.031
rmsea.ci.upper 0.040
srmr 0.041
aic 42159.518
bic 42724.830 
cat("Chi2/df =", round(fm_sem["chisq"] / fm_sem["df"], 3), "\n")
## Chi2/df = 1.693

The model achieves CFI ≈ 0.97-0.98, RMSEA ≈ 0.04, SRMR ≈ 0.04 and Chi²/df ≈ 1.7, which is excellent fit across all values.

3.3 Structural Paths – Question 2

The tables below present the standardised path coefficients from each image dimension to the two mediators, and from the mediators to the two outcomes. These results answer Question 2: are the mechanisms driving Satisfaction and Affective Commitment similar?

For this, we need to understand the beta values, which are the standardised path coefficients. They express the expected change in the mediator/outcome (in standard deviation units) for a one standard deviation increase in the predictor, holding all other predictors constant. Because everything is on the same scale, betas from different predictors can be compared directly to assess relative importance. The SE is the standard error of that estimate, measuring uncertainty in the estimated path. A smaller SE relative to the Beta indicates a more precisely estimated relationship. The z-value is the ratio β / SE, and under the null hypothesis of no effect it follows an approximately standard normal distribution. Values beyond +-1.96 correspond to p < .05.

A negative beta indicates that higher scores on the predictor are associated with lower scores on the mediator/outcome. In this context, if an image dimension had a negative beta on Satisfaction, it would mean that customers rating the store higher on that dimension actually report lower satisfaction on average.

In the table below, you can see the betas from the latent factors to the mediators, and if they are significant or not.

# beta from latent factors -> mediators
med_paths <- standardizedSolution(fit_sem) %>%
  filter(op == "~", lhs %in% c("Satisfaction","AffectiveCommitment")) %>%
  transmute(Mediator = lhs, Image = str_remove(rhs, "Image_"),
            Beta = round(est.std, 3), SE = round(se, 3),
            z = round(z, 2), p = round(pvalue, 3),
            Sig = ifelse(pvalue < .05, "sig", "-")) %>%
  arrange(Mediator, p)

med_paths %>%
  kbl(caption = "Standardised paths: Image dimensions -> Mediators") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover")) %>%
  column_spec(7, bold = TRUE,
              color = ifelse(med_paths$Sig == "sig", "#27ae60", "#c0392b"))
Standardised paths: Image dimensions -> Mediators
Mediator Image Beta SE z p Sig
AffectiveCommitment Atmosphere 0.377 0.062 6.06 0.000 sig
AffectiveCommitment FrenchIdentity 0.171 0.058 2.94 0.003 sig
AffectiveCommitment Assortment 0.346 0.119 2.91 0.004 sig
AffectiveCommitment Luxury -0.208 0.083 -2.50 0.012 sig
AffectiveCommitment Gourmet 0.037 0.060 0.62 0.533
AffectiveCommitment Trendy -0.036 0.072 -0.50 0.615
AffectiveCommitment Decoration -0.024 0.060 -0.40 0.688
Satisfaction Assortment 0.626 0.138 4.54 0.000 sig
Satisfaction Gourmet 0.152 0.077 1.97 0.049 sig
Satisfaction FrenchIdentity 0.095 0.061 1.55 0.121
Satisfaction Luxury -0.139 0.095 -1.46 0.143
Satisfaction Decoration -0.066 0.060 -1.10 0.271
Satisfaction Atmosphere 0.018 0.070 0.25 0.802
Satisfaction Trendy 0.013 0.082 0.16 0.876

From the table, we can read the following:

AffectiveCommitment : Has four significative latent factors, which are Atmosphere, Assortment, Frenchidentity, and Luxury, in this order. Satisfaction : Two significative latent factors, which are Assortment and Gourmet, in thos order.

We can also have a look at the mediator-to-outcome paths with the full mediation model. The table shows that they are all significative and how strongly each mediator predicts each outcome. These are regression coefficients (betas), telling us the direction and magnitude of each path, with significance tests.

# mediator to outcome betas for full mediation
out_paths <- standardizedSolution(fit_sem) %>%
  filter(op == "~",
         lhs %in% c("Repurchase","CoCreation"),
         rhs %in% c("Satisfaction","AffectiveCommitment")) %>%
  transmute(Outcome=lhs, Predictor=rhs,
            Beta=round(est.std,3), SE=round(se,3),
            z=round(z,2), p=round(pvalue,3),
            Sig=ifelse(pvalue < .05, "sig", "-"))

out_paths %>%
  kbl(caption = "Standardised paths: Mediators -> Outcomes") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover")) %>%
  column_spec(7, bold=TRUE,
              color=ifelse(out_paths$Sig=="sig","#27ae60","#c0392b"))
Standardised paths: Mediators -> Outcomes
Outcome Predictor Beta SE z p Sig
Repurchase Satisfaction 0.313 0.055 5.67 0 sig
Repurchase AffectiveCommitment 0.380 0.049 7.69 0 sig
CoCreation Satisfaction -0.239 0.056 -4.30 0 sig
CoCreation AffectiveCommitment 0.399 0.050 7.97 0 sig

The table below gives the R², or Variance explained. This is how much of each construct’s total variance is accounted for by the model overall.

#rsquare inspection
r2 <- inspect(fit_sem, "r2")
data.frame(Construct=names(r2), R2=round(as.numeric(r2),3)) %>%
  filter(Construct %in% c("Satisfaction","AffectiveCommitment","Repurchase","CoCreation")) %>%
  kbl(caption = "Variance explained (R2) for endogenous constructs") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"), full_width = FALSE)
Variance explained (R2) for endogenous constructs
Construct R2
Satisfaction 0.415
AffectiveCommitment 0.369
Repurchase 0.321
CoCreation 0.153

The barplot below summarizes the findings for the mediators. It reveals two distinct mediator profiles. Assortment is the only dimension that drives both mediators strongly: it dominates Satisfaction (beta approx equal to 0.63) and contributes meaningfully to Affective Commitment as well. Atmosphere and FrenchIdentity are primarily emotional drivers, predicting Affective Commitment but not Satisfaction. Gourmet does the opposite. It matters for Satisfaction but not for Commitment. Luxury is the only negative predictor, suppressing both mediators. Decoration and Trendy are non-significant in both cases.

# barplot
med_paths %>%
  mutate(Alpha = ifelse(Sig == "sig", 1, 0.35)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(Image, Beta), y = Beta, fill = Mediator, alpha = I(Alpha))) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(0.8), width = 0.72) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "grey40") +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Satisfaction"="#2980b9","AffectiveCommitment"="#8e44ad"),
                    name = NULL) +
  labs(title    = "Image Dimensions -> Mediators: Standardised Beta",
       subtitle = "Transparent = non-significant (p > .05)",
       x = NULL, y = "Beta") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"), legend.position = "top")

Finally, the diagram below shows the full mediation model.

# diagram showing the full model
semPaths(fit_sem, what="std", whatLabels="std", layout="tree2", rotation=2,
         style="lisrel", residuals=FALSE, intercepts=FALSE, exoCov=FALSE,
         nCharNodes=5, sizeLat=11, sizeMan=5, edge.label.cex=0.6,
         label.cex=0.8, curvePivot=FALSE, cardinal=FALSE, weighted=FALSE,
         groups="latents", pastel=TRUE, mar=c(1,1,2,1),
         main="Full Mediation SEM - Standardised Path Coefficients")

SEM Full Mediation

Q2 – Are the mechanisms driving Satisfaction and Affective Commitment similar?

No, the pathways differ meaningfully:

  • Drivers of Satisfaction: Assortment is the dominant predictor. Gourmet adds a smaller positive contribution. These two image dimensions reflect what the store offers (range, food curation), and they predict the cognitive evaluation captured by Satisfaction.
  • Drivers of Affective Commitment: Atmosphere is the dominant predictor, French Identity also contributes positively. These are how the store feels dimensions, which build the emotional bond captured by Affective Commitment.
  • Assortment is the most versatile dimension: it predicts Satisfaction strongly and also contributes meaningfully to Affective Commitment.
  • Luxury is a suppressor: The standardised path from Luxury to both mediators is negative once the other six image dimensions are controlled for. Customers who score the store especially high on luxury imagery, beyond what their other perceptions would predict, report somewhat lower satisfaction and weaker emotional attachment.
  • Trendy and Decoration do not significantly predict either mediator at conventional levels.

Mediation confirmed: (Already for Q3) Both mediators significantly predict both outcomes. Co-Creation is more strongly driven by Affective Commitment, and Repurchase is more balanced between both mediators.

3.4 Decomposition of Indirect Effects with the full mediated model

The next mode is the same model as before, but re-specified with labels so we can better extract and report the indirect effects.

#fully mediated model
sem_delta_model <- '
  Image_Decoration     =~ Im4  + Im3  + Im5
  Image_Assortment     =~ Im1  + Im2  + Im15
  Image_Atmosphere     =~ Im22 + Im20 + Im21
  Image_Luxury         =~ Im12 + Im13
  Image_FrenchIdentity =~ Im7  + Im6
  Image_Gourmet        =~ Im14 + Im10
  Image_Trendy         =~ Im17 + Im18
  Im1 ~~ Im2

  Satisfaction        =~ SAT_1  + SAT_2  + SAT_3
  AffectiveCommitment =~ COM_A1 + COM_A2 + COM_A3 + COM_A4
  Repurchase          =~ C_REP1 + C_REP2 + C_REP3
  CoCreation          =~ C_CR1  + C_CR3  + C_CR4

  # Labeled structural paths
  Satisfaction        ~ a1*Image_Atmosphere + a2*Image_Luxury + a3*Image_Assortment +
                        a4*Image_Decoration + a5*Image_FrenchIdentity +
                        a6*Image_Gourmet    + a7*Image_Trendy
  AffectiveCommitment ~ c1*Image_Atmosphere + c2*Image_Luxury + c3*Image_Assortment +
                        c4*Image_Decoration + c5*Image_FrenchIdentity +
                        c6*Image_Gourmet    + c7*Image_Trendy
  Repurchase  ~ b1*Satisfaction + b2*AffectiveCommitment
  CoCreation  ~ d1*Satisfaction + d2*AffectiveCommitment

  # Indirect effects (total via both mediators)
  ind_Atm_Rep := (a1*b1) + (c1*b2)
  ind_Lux_Rep := (a2*b1) + (c2*b2)
  ind_Ass_Rep := (a3*b1) + (c3*b2)
  ind_Dec_Rep := (a4*b1) + (c4*b2)
  ind_Fre_Rep := (a5*b1) + (c5*b2)
  ind_Gou_Rep := (a6*b1) + (c6*b2)
  ind_Tre_Rep := (a7*b1) + (c7*b2)

  ind_Atm_CC  := (a1*d1) + (c1*d2)
  ind_Lux_CC  := (a2*d1) + (c2*d2)
  ind_Ass_CC  := (a3*d1) + (c3*d2)
  ind_Dec_CC  := (a4*d1) + (c4*d2)
  ind_Fre_CC  := (a5*d1) + (c5*d2)
  ind_Gou_CC  := (a6*d1) + (c6*d2)
  ind_Tre_CC  := (a7*d1) + (c7*d2)

  # Pathway decomposition: via Satisfaction only
  via_sat_Atm_Rep := a1*b1;  via_sat_Atm_CC := a1*d1
  via_sat_Lux_Rep := a2*b1;  via_sat_Lux_CC := a2*d1
  via_sat_Ass_Rep := a3*b1;  via_sat_Ass_CC := a3*d1
  via_sat_Dec_Rep := a4*b1;  via_sat_Dec_CC := a4*d1
  via_sat_Fre_Rep := a5*b1;  via_sat_Fre_CC := a5*d1
  via_sat_Gou_Rep := a6*b1;  via_sat_Gou_CC := a6*d1
  via_sat_Tre_Rep := a7*b1;  via_sat_Tre_CC := a7*d1

  # Pathway decomposition: via AffectiveCommitment only
  via_com_Atm_Rep := c1*b2;  via_com_Atm_CC := c1*d2
  via_com_Lux_Rep := c2*b2;  via_com_Lux_CC := c2*d2
  via_com_Ass_Rep := c3*b2;  via_com_Ass_CC := c3*d2
  via_com_Dec_Rep := c4*b2;  via_com_Dec_CC := c4*d2
  via_com_Fre_Rep := c5*b2;  via_com_Fre_CC := c5*d2
  via_com_Gou_Rep := c6*b2;  via_com_Gou_CC := c6*d2
  via_com_Tre_Rep := c7*b2;  via_com_Tre_CC := c7*d2
'

fit_delta <- cfa(sem_delta_model, data = data_raw,
                 estimator = "MLR", missing = "fiml")

# Extract all := results
delta_params <- parameterEstimates(fit_delta, standardized = TRUE) %>%
  filter(op == ":=")

delta_indirect <- delta_params %>%
  filter(grepl("^ind_", label)) %>%
  transmute(
    Effect   = label,
    Std_Est  = round(std.all, 3),
    SE       = round(se, 3),
    CI_Lower = round(ci.lower, 3),
    CI_Upper = round(ci.upper, 3),
    p        = round(pvalue, 3),
    Sig      = ifelse(pvalue < .05, "YES", "no")
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(abs(Std_Est)))

delta_indirect %>%
  kbl(caption = "Indirect effects with 95% CIs (N = 553, FIML)",
      col.names = c("Indirect Effect","Std. Est.","SE","95% CI Low","95% CI High","p","Sig?")) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
  column_spec(7, bold = TRUE,
              color = ifelse(delta_indirect$Sig == "YES", "#27ae60", "#c0392b"))
Indirect effects with 95% CIs (N = 553, FIML)
Indirect Effect Std. Est. SE 95% CI Low 95% CI High p Sig?
ind_Ass_Rep 0.328 0.055 0.085 0.299 0.000 YES
ind_Atm_Rep 0.149 0.021 0.026 0.107 0.001 YES
ind_Atm_CC 0.146 0.038 0.107 0.257 0.000 YES
ind_Lux_Rep -0.123 0.037 -0.151 -0.007 0.032 YES
ind_Fre_Rep 0.095 0.019 0.012 0.087 0.010 YES
ind_Gou_Rep 0.062 0.030 -0.015 0.104 0.142 no
ind_Lux_CC -0.050 0.050 -0.186 0.008 0.073 no
ind_Fre_CC 0.046 0.033 0.002 0.130 0.043 YES
ind_Dec_Rep -0.030 0.016 -0.045 0.018 0.393 no
ind_Gou_CC -0.021 0.049 -0.138 0.053 0.383 no
ind_Tre_CC -0.017 0.034 -0.090 0.042 0.480 no
ind_Ass_CC -0.011 0.076 -0.167 0.131 0.815 no
ind_Tre_Rep -0.010 0.023 -0.050 0.040 0.835 no
ind_Dec_CC 0.006 0.029 -0.049 0.065 0.791 no

The table above gives indirect effects only: how much each image dimension influences the outcomes through the two mediators combined, and their significance. The plot below is a summary of the results.

delta_indirect %>%
  mutate(
    Outcome   = ifelse(grepl("Rep$", Effect), "Repurchase", "CoCreation"),
    Dimension = Effect %>%
                  str_remove("^ind_") %>%
                  str_remove("_Rep$|_CC$") %>%
                  recode("Atm"="Atmosphere","Lux"="Luxury","Ass"="Assortment",
                         "Dec"="Decoration","Fre"="FrenchIdentity",
                         "Gou"="Gourmet","Tre"="Trendy"),
    Sig = factor(Sig, levels = c("YES","no"))
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(Dimension, Std_Est), y = Std_Est, fill = Sig)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "grey40") +
  coord_flip() +
  facet_wrap(~Outcome) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("YES" = "#27ae60", "no" = "#bdc3c7"),
                    labels = c("YES" = "Significant (p < .05)",
                               "no"  = "Non-significant"),
                    name = NULL) +
  labs(title    = "Indirect Effects — Standardised Estimates",
       subtitle = "N = 553 (FIML)  |  Green = significant (p < .05)",
       x = NULL, y = "Standardised Indirect Effect") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
        legend.position = "top")

If the 95% CI does not contain zero (Sig = YES), the indirect effect is statistically significant and mediation is confirmed for that path.

What we see from the plot:

  • For Repurchase, three dimensions transmit significant indirect effects: Assortment is by far the strongest (beta = 0.328), followed by Atmosphere (0.149) and FrenchIdentity (0.095). Luxury has a significant negative indirect effect (−0.123), meaning it actually suppresses repurchase intention through the mediators.

  • For CoCreation, the picture is much weaker. Only Atmosphere (0.146) and FrenchIdentity (0.046) reach significance. Assortment, despite being the dominant driver of Repurchase, has a near-zero and non-significant indirect effect on CoCreation (−0.011). This makes sense given what we saw earlier: Assortment drives Satisfaction strongly, but Satisfaction has a negative path to CoCreation, which cancels out its positive contribution via Affective Commitment.

In the plot below, you can see the exact decomposition between both mediators.

decode_image <- function(x) {
  x %>%
    str_remove("^(ind|via_sat|via_com)_") %>%
    str_remove("_Rep$|_CC$") %>%
    recode("Atm"="Atmosphere","Lux"="Luxury","Ass"="Assortment",
           "Dec"="Decoration","Fre"="FrenchIdentity","Gou"="Gourmet","Tre"="Trendy")
}

via_sat_tbl <- delta_params %>%
  filter(grepl("^via_sat_", label)) %>%
  transmute(
    Outcome          = ifelse(grepl("_Rep$", label), "Repurchase", "CoCreation"),
    Image            = decode_image(label),
    Via_Satisfaction = round(std.all, 3)
  )

via_com_tbl <- delta_params %>%
  filter(grepl("^via_com_", label)) %>%
  transmute(
    Outcome        = ifelse(grepl("_Rep$", label), "Repurchase", "CoCreation"),
    Image          = decode_image(label),
    Via_Commitment = round(std.all, 3)
  )

ind_tbl <- delta_params %>%
  filter(grepl("^ind_", label)) %>%
  transmute(
    Outcome      = ifelse(grepl("_Rep$", label), "Repurchase", "CoCreation"),
    Image        = decode_image(label),
    Indirect_std = round(std.all, 3)
  )

pathway_tbl <- ind_tbl %>%
  left_join(via_sat_tbl, by = c("Outcome", "Image")) %>%
  left_join(via_com_tbl, by = c("Outcome", "Image")) %>%
  arrange(Outcome, desc(abs(Indirect_std)))

pathway_tbl %>%
  pivot_longer(c(Via_Satisfaction, Via_Commitment),
               names_to = "Pathway", values_to = "Effect") %>%
  mutate(Pathway = recode(Pathway,
           "Via_Satisfaction" = "via Satisfaction",
           "Via_Commitment"   = "via Commitment")) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(Image, Effect), y = Effect, fill = Pathway)) +
  geom_col(position = position_stack(), width = 0.75) +
  coord_flip() +
  facet_wrap(~Outcome) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("via Satisfaction"="#3498db","via Commitment"="#9b59b6"),
                    name = NULL) +
  labs(title    = "Pathway Decomposition: via Satisfaction vs. via Commitment",
       subtitle = "Stacked = indirect effect  |  Colour = mediating channel",
       x = NULL, y = "Standardised Indirect Effect") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
        legend.position = "bottom")

4. Partially mediated model and Total Effects – Question 3

Here, the next model (fit_full) contains all labeled paths, e.g. indirect, direct and total.

# Helper vectors used throughout section 4
images    <- c("Image_Decoration","Image_Assortment","Image_Atmosphere",
               "Image_Luxury","Image_FrenchIdentity","Image_Gourmet","Image_Trendy")
mediators <- c("Satisfaction","AffectiveCommitment")
outcomes  <- c("Repurchase","CoCreation")

4.1 Direct Paths only

We fit the model below.

#partially mediated model
sem_full_model <- '
  Image_Decoration     =~ Im4  + Im3  + Im5
  Image_Assortment     =~ Im1  + Im2  + Im15
  Image_Atmosphere     =~ Im22 + Im20 + Im21
  Image_Luxury         =~ Im12 + Im13
  Image_FrenchIdentity =~ Im7  + Im6
  Image_Gourmet        =~ Im14 + Im10
  Image_Trendy         =~ Im17 + Im18
  Im1 ~~ Im2

  Satisfaction        =~ SAT_1  + SAT_2  + SAT_3
  AffectiveCommitment =~ COM_A1 + COM_A2 + COM_A3 + COM_A4
  Repurchase          =~ C_REP1 + C_REP2 + C_REP3
  CoCreation          =~ C_CR1  + C_CR3  + C_CR4

  # Labeled mediation paths
  Satisfaction        ~ a1*Image_Atmosphere + a2*Image_Luxury + a3*Image_Assortment +
                        a4*Image_Decoration + a5*Image_FrenchIdentity +
                        a6*Image_Gourmet    + a7*Image_Trendy
  AffectiveCommitment ~ c1*Image_Atmosphere + c2*Image_Luxury + c3*Image_Assortment +
                        c4*Image_Decoration + c5*Image_FrenchIdentity +
                        c6*Image_Gourmet    + c7*Image_Trendy
  Repurchase ~ b1*Satisfaction + b2*AffectiveCommitment
  CoCreation ~ d1*Satisfaction + d2*AffectiveCommitment

  # Labeled direct paths
  Repurchase ~ e1*Image_Atmosphere + e2*Image_Luxury + e3*Image_Assortment +
               e4*Image_Decoration + e5*Image_FrenchIdentity +
               e6*Image_Gourmet    + e7*Image_Trendy
  CoCreation ~ f1*Image_Atmosphere + f2*Image_Luxury + f3*Image_Assortment +
               f4*Image_Decoration + f5*Image_FrenchIdentity +
               f6*Image_Gourmet    + f7*Image_Trendy

  # Indirect effects
  ind_Atm_Rep := (a1*b1) + (c1*b2);  ind_Atm_CC := (a1*d1) + (c1*d2)
  ind_Lux_Rep := (a2*b1) + (c2*b2);  ind_Lux_CC := (a2*d1) + (c2*d2)
  ind_Ass_Rep := (a3*b1) + (c3*b2);  ind_Ass_CC := (a3*d1) + (c3*d2)
  ind_Dec_Rep := (a4*b1) + (c4*b2);  ind_Dec_CC := (a4*d1) + (c4*d2)
  ind_Fre_Rep := (a5*b1) + (c5*b2);  ind_Fre_CC := (a5*d1) + (c5*d2)
  ind_Gou_Rep := (a6*b1) + (c6*b2);  ind_Gou_CC := (a6*d1) + (c6*d2)
  ind_Tre_Rep := (a7*b1) + (c7*b2);  ind_Tre_CC := (a7*d1) + (c7*d2)

  # Direct effects
  dir_Atm_Rep := e1;  dir_Atm_CC := f1
  dir_Lux_Rep := e2;  dir_Lux_CC := f2
  dir_Ass_Rep := e3;  dir_Ass_CC := f3
  dir_Dec_Rep := e4;  dir_Dec_CC := f4
  dir_Fre_Rep := e5;  dir_Fre_CC := f5
  dir_Gou_Rep := e6;  dir_Gou_CC := f6
  dir_Tre_Rep := e7;  dir_Tre_CC := f7

  # Total effects = indirect + direct (exactly as in class: t := c + a*b)
  tot_Atm_Rep := (a1*b1) + (c1*b2) + e1;  tot_Atm_CC := (a1*d1) + (c1*d2) + f1
  tot_Lux_Rep := (a2*b1) + (c2*b2) + e2;  tot_Lux_CC := (a2*d1) + (c2*d2) + f2
  tot_Ass_Rep := (a3*b1) + (c3*b2) + e3;  tot_Ass_CC := (a3*d1) + (c3*d2) + f3
  tot_Dec_Rep := (a4*b1) + (c4*b2) + e4;  tot_Dec_CC := (a4*d1) + (c4*d2) + f4
  tot_Fre_Rep := (a5*b1) + (c5*b2) + e5;  tot_Fre_CC := (a5*d1) + (c5*d2) + f5
  tot_Gou_Rep := (a6*b1) + (c6*b2) + e6;  tot_Gou_CC := (a6*d1) + (c6*d2) + f6
  tot_Tre_Rep := (a7*b1) + (c7*b2) + e7;  tot_Tre_CC := (a7*d1) + (c7*d2) + f7
'

fit_boot_full <- cfa(sem_full_model, data = data_raw,
                     estimator = "MLR", missing = "fiml")

# Extract all results
full_params <- parameterEstimates(fit_boot_full, standardized = TRUE)

# Fit comparison
fit_full_compare <- data.frame(
  Model   = c("Full Mediation (fit_sem)", "Partial Mediation (fit_boot_full)"),
  Chi2_df = round(c(fitMeasures(fit_sem,       "chisq")/fitMeasures(fit_sem,       "df"),
                    fitMeasures(fit_boot_full, "chisq")/fitMeasures(fit_boot_full, "df")), 3),
  CFI     = round(c(fitMeasures(fit_sem,       "cfi"),   fitMeasures(fit_boot_full, "cfi")),   3),
  RMSEA   = round(c(fitMeasures(fit_sem,       "rmsea"), fitMeasures(fit_boot_full, "rmsea")), 3),
  SRMR    = round(c(fitMeasures(fit_sem,       "srmr"),  fitMeasures(fit_boot_full, "srmr")),  3),
  AIC     = round(c(fitMeasures(fit_sem,       "aic"),   fitMeasures(fit_boot_full, "aic")),   1)
)

fit_full_compare %>%
  kbl(caption = "Full vs Partial Mediation — Fit Comparison") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
  row_spec(1, bold = TRUE, background = "#d5f5e3")
Full vs Partial Mediation — Fit Comparison
Model Chi2_df CFI RMSEA SRMR AIC
Full Mediation (fit_sem) 1.693 0.977 0.035 0.041 42159.5
Partial Mediation (fit_boot_full) 1.710 0.977 0.036 0.039 42169.8

In the table above, we compare the full vs partial mediation. The fit comparison seems to show that the full mediation model is preferable. Both models achieve excellent fit by all indices (CFI = 0.977, RMSEA ≈ 0.035–0.036, SRMR ≈ 0.039–0.041) However, the full mediation model has a lower AIC (42159.5 vs 42169.8), indicating that it achieves a better balance between fit and parsimony. Still, below we continue to analyze the partialy mediated model.

First, we only have a look at the direct paths. They are described in the table below. Their significance can be seen in the next table.

# direct paths
direct_paths <- full_params %>%
  filter(op == ":=", grepl("^dir_", label)) %>%
  transmute(
    Outcome  = ifelse(grepl("_Rep$", label), "Repurchase", "CoCreation"),
    Image    = label %>%
                 str_remove("^dir_") %>%
                 str_remove("_Rep$|_CC$") %>%
                 recode("Atm"="Atmosphere","Lux"="Luxury","Ass"="Assortment",
                        "Dec"="Decoration","Fre"="FrenchIdentity",
                        "Gou"="Gourmet","Tre"="Trendy"),
    Beta_std = round(std.all, 3),
    Beta_est = round(est, 3),
    SE       = round(se, 3),
    CI_Lower = round(ci.lower, 3),
    CI_Upper = round(ci.upper, 3),
    p        = round(pvalue, 3),
    Sig      = ifelse(pvalue < .05, "YES", "no")
  ) %>%
  arrange(Outcome, desc(abs(Beta_std)))

# Table 1: Standardised estimates — ranking
direct_paths %>%
  select(Outcome, Image, Beta_std) %>%
  kbl(caption = "Direct Paths — Standardised estimates (ranking)",
      col.names = c("Outcome","Image Dimension","Std. Beta")) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
  column_spec(3, bold = TRUE)
Direct Paths — Standardised estimates (ranking)
Outcome Image Dimension Std. Beta
CoCreation Atmosphere 0.126
CoCreation Assortment -0.101
CoCreation Luxury 0.082
CoCreation FrenchIdentity -0.075
CoCreation Gourmet -0.046
CoCreation Decoration -0.036
CoCreation Trendy 0.022
Repurchase Luxury 0.126
Repurchase Assortment -0.126
Repurchase Atmosphere 0.095
Repurchase FrenchIdentity -0.055
Repurchase Gourmet 0.048
Repurchase Trendy -0.018
Repurchase Decoration 0.016 
# Table 2: Unstandardised estimates with CIs — significance
direct_paths %>%
  select(Outcome, Image, Beta_est, SE, CI_Lower, CI_Upper, p, Sig) %>%
  kbl(caption = "Direct Paths — Unstandardised estimates with 95% CIs (significance)",
      col.names = c("Outcome","Image Dimension","Est.","SE","CI Low","CI High","p","Sig")) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
  column_spec(8, bold = TRUE,
              color = ifelse(direct_paths$Sig == "YES", "#27ae60", "#c0392b"))
Direct Paths — Unstandardised estimates with 95% CIs (significance)
Outcome Image Dimension Est. SE CI Low CI High p Sig
CoCreation Atmosphere 0.157 0.088 -0.015 0.329 0.073 no
CoCreation Assortment -0.164 0.187 -0.530 0.203 0.381 no
CoCreation Luxury 0.145 0.127 -0.104 0.394 0.253 no
CoCreation FrenchIdentity -0.108 0.095 -0.294 0.077 0.252 no
CoCreation Gourmet -0.092 0.131 -0.350 0.165 0.483 no
CoCreation Decoration -0.046 0.085 -0.213 0.121 0.591 no
CoCreation Trendy 0.030 0.089 -0.144 0.205 0.734 no
Repurchase Luxury 0.081 0.046 -0.009 0.171 0.077 no
Repurchase Assortment -0.074 0.063 -0.198 0.050 0.243 no
Repurchase Atmosphere 0.043 0.028 -0.013 0.098 0.134 no
Repurchase FrenchIdentity -0.029 0.027 -0.082 0.024 0.287 no
Repurchase Gourmet 0.035 0.043 -0.049 0.118 0.420 no
Repurchase Trendy -0.009 0.029 -0.065 0.047 0.761 no
Repurchase Decoration 0.007 0.025 -0.041 0.056 0.761 no

All direct effects are small in magnitude, and none stands out as a dominant predictor.

# Standardised effects only 
direct_paths %>%
  mutate(Alpha = ifelse(Sig == "YES", 1, 0.35)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(Image, Beta_std), y = Beta_std,
             fill = Outcome, alpha = I(Alpha))) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(0.8), width = 0.72) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "grey40") +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Repurchase"="#2980b9","CoCreation"="#27ae60"), name = NULL) +
  labs(title    = "Direct Paths: Image Dimensions → Outcomes",
       subtitle = "Standardised effects  |  Transparent = non-significant (p > .05)",
       x = NULL, y = "Standardised Beta") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"), legend.position = "top")

Non-significant direct paths confirm that the effects of brand image seem to be mostly mediated through Satisfaction and Affective Commitment.

4.2 Total Effects = Indirect + Direct

Now we have a look at Total effect = indirect paths + direct paths.

total_full <- full_params %>%
  filter(op == ":=", grepl("^tot_", label)) %>%
  transmute(
    Outcome   = ifelse(grepl("_Rep$", label), "Repurchase", "CoCreation"),
    Image     = label %>%
                  str_remove("^tot_") %>%
                  str_remove("_Rep$|_CC$") %>%
                  recode("Atm"="Atmosphere","Lux"="Luxury","Ass"="Assortment",
                         "Dec"="Decoration","Fre"="FrenchIdentity",
                         "Gou"="Gourmet","Tre"="Trendy"),
    Total_std = round(std.all, 3),
    Total_est = round(est, 3),
    SE        = round(se, 3),
    CI_Lower  = round(ci.lower, 3),
    CI_Upper  = round(ci.upper, 3),
    p         = round(pvalue, 3),
    Sig       = ifelse(pvalue < .05, "YES", "no")
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    full_params %>%
      filter(op == ":=", grepl("^ind_", label)) %>%
      transmute(
        Outcome      = ifelse(grepl("_Rep$", label), "Repurchase", "CoCreation"),
        Image        = label %>%
                         str_remove("^ind_") %>%
                         str_remove("_Rep$|_CC$") %>%
                         recode("Atm"="Atmosphere","Lux"="Luxury","Ass"="Assortment",
                                "Dec"="Decoration","Fre"="FrenchIdentity",
                                "Gou"="Gourmet","Tre"="Trendy"),
        Indirect_std = round(std.all, 3)
      ),
    by = c("Outcome","Image")
  ) %>%
  left_join(direct_paths %>% select(Outcome, Image, Direct_std = Beta_std),
            by = c("Outcome","Image")) %>%
  arrange(Outcome, desc(abs(Total_std)))

# Table 1: Standardised estimates — ranking
total_full %>%
  select(Outcome, Image, Indirect_std, Direct_std, Total_std) %>%
  kbl(caption = "Total Effects = Indirect + Direct — Standardised estimates (ranking)",
      col.names = c("Outcome","Image","Indirect (std)","Direct (std)","Total (std)")) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover","condensed"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
  column_spec(5, bold = TRUE)
Total Effects = Indirect + Direct — Standardised estimates (ranking)
Outcome Image Indirect (std) Direct (std) Total (std)
CoCreation Atmosphere 0.139 0.126 0.265
CoCreation Assortment 0.015 -0.101 -0.086
CoCreation Gourmet -0.014 -0.046 -0.061
CoCreation Decoration 0.004 -0.036 -0.032
CoCreation Luxury -0.057 0.082 0.025
CoCreation FrenchIdentity 0.050 -0.075 -0.025
CoCreation Trendy -0.017 0.022 0.005
Repurchase Atmosphere 0.139 0.095 0.234
Repurchase Assortment 0.331 -0.126 0.205
Repurchase Gourmet 0.059 0.048 0.107
Repurchase FrenchIdentity 0.096 -0.055 0.041
Repurchase Trendy -0.009 -0.018 -0.026
Repurchase Decoration -0.030 0.016 -0.014
Repurchase Luxury -0.127 0.126 -0.001 
# Table 2: Unstandardised estimates with CIs — significance
total_full %>%
  select(Outcome, Image, Total_est, SE, CI_Lower, CI_Upper, p, Sig) %>%
  kbl(caption = "Total Effects — Unstandardised estimates with 95% CIs (significance)",
      col.names = c("Outcome","Image","Est.","SE","CI Low","CI High","p","Sig")) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover","condensed"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
  column_spec(8, bold = TRUE,
              color = ifelse(total_full$Sig == "YES", "#27ae60", "#c0392b"))
Total Effects — Unstandardised estimates with 95% CIs (significance)
Outcome Image Est. SE CI Low CI High p Sig
CoCreation Atmosphere 0.330 0.084 0.166 0.494 0.000 YES
CoCreation Assortment -0.139 0.163 -0.458 0.180 0.393 no
CoCreation Gourmet -0.121 0.138 -0.392 0.150 0.381 no
CoCreation Decoration -0.040 0.085 -0.208 0.127 0.636 no
CoCreation Luxury 0.044 0.134 -0.218 0.306 0.741 no
CoCreation FrenchIdentity -0.035 0.095 -0.222 0.151 0.710 no
CoCreation Trendy 0.007 0.095 -0.179 0.194 0.938 no
Repurchase Atmosphere 0.105 0.032 0.042 0.167 0.001 YES
Repurchase Assortment 0.120 0.065 -0.007 0.247 0.065 no
Repurchase Gourmet 0.077 0.054 -0.029 0.183 0.154 no
Repurchase FrenchIdentity 0.021 0.030 -0.038 0.080 0.483 no
Repurchase Trendy -0.013 0.035 -0.082 0.056 0.715 no
Repurchase Decoration -0.006 0.027 -0.059 0.046 0.815 no
Repurchase Luxury -0.001 0.055 -0.109 0.108 0.993 no

From the tables above we can see that: The total effect (indirect + direct) reveals that for Repurchase, Atmosphere (0.234) and Assortment (0.205) are the strongest positive drivers overall, while Luxury nearly cancels out (−0.001) as its positive direct and negative indirect effects offset each other. For CoCreation, Atmosphere is again the dominant dimension (0.265), while most other dimensions have near-zero or slightly negative total effects, reflecting the competing directions of their indirect and direct components.

For the significance, only two total effects reach statistical significance: Atmosphere on both CoCreation (p < .001) and Repurchase (p = .001).

# Standardised effects only — no CI bars
total_full %>%
  pivot_longer(c(Indirect_std, Direct_std), names_to = "Type", values_to = "Effect") %>%
  mutate(Type = recode(Type, "Indirect_std" = "Indirect", "Direct_std" = "Direct")) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(Image, abs(Total_std)), y = Effect, fill = Type)) +
  geom_col(position = position_stack(), width = 0.75) +
  coord_flip() +
  facet_wrap(~Outcome) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Indirect"="#3498db","Direct"="#e74c3c"), name = NULL) +
  labs(title    = "Total Effects = Indirect + Direct",
       subtitle = "Standardised effects  |  Blue = indirect, Red = direct",
       x = NULL, y = "Standardised Effect") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
        legend.position = "bottom")

The stacked bars decompose each dimension’s total effect into its indirect (blue) and direct (red) components, making the suppression patterns immediately visible.

Below, a diagram describes the partially mediated model that we have.

semPaths(fit_boot_full, what="std", whatLabels="std", layout="tree2", rotation=2,
         style="lisrel", residuals=FALSE, intercepts=FALSE, exoCov=FALSE,
         nCharNodes=5, sizeLat=11, sizeMan=5, edge.label.cex=0.55,
         label.cex=0.8, curvePivot=FALSE, cardinal=FALSE, weighted=FALSE,
         groups="latents", pastel=TRUE, mar=c(1,1,2,1),
         main="Full Causal Model - Standardised Path Coefficients (Indirect + Direct)")

Full Causal Model with Direct + Indirect Paths 

q3_ranking <- total_full %>%
  group_by(Outcome) %>%
  arrange(desc(Total_std), .by_group = TRUE) %>%
  summarise(Ranking = paste(Image, sprintf("(%.3f)", Total_std), collapse = " > "),
            .groups = "drop")

q3_ranking %>%
  kbl(caption = "Q3: Ranking of image dimensions by standardised total effect",
      col.names = c("Outcome","Ranking (highest -> lowest total effect)")) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"), full_width = FALSE)
Q3: Ranking of image dimensions by standardised total effect
Outcome Ranking (highest -> lowest total effect)
CoCreation Atmosphere (0.265) > Luxury (0.025) > Trendy (0.005) > FrenchIdentity (-0.025) > Decoration (-0.032) > Gourmet (-0.061) > Assortment (-0.086)
Repurchase Atmosphere (0.234) > Assortment (0.205) > Gourmet (0.107) > FrenchIdentity (0.041) > Luxury (-0.001) > Decoration (-0.014) > Trendy (-0.026)

The ranking above is generated directly from the total effects. Dimensions flagged as significant (p < .05) represent the most defensible drivers for managerial action.

5. Robustness Check: Model Generalisation

5.1 Robustness — Fit Comparison

The robustness SEM adds all items removed during EFA/CFA (Im8, Im9, Im11, Im16 and Im19) as standalone observed predictors directly onto the mediators and outcomes, without assigning them to any latent factor. This tests whether any of these excluded items carries unique predictive information beyond the seven-factor structure. For this, we use the full mediation model.

# robustness check
sem_robust <- '
  Image_Decoration     =~ Im4  + Im3  + Im5
  Image_Assortment     =~ Im1  + Im2  + Im15
  Image_Atmosphere     =~ Im20 + Im22 + Im21
  Image_Luxury         =~ Im12 + Im13
  Image_FrenchIdentity =~ Im7  + Im6
  Image_Gourmet        =~ Im14 + Im10
  Image_Trendy         =~ Im17 + Im18
  Im1 ~~ Im2

  Satisfaction        =~ SAT_1  + SAT_2  + SAT_3
  AffectiveCommitment =~ COM_A1 + COM_A2 + COM_A3 + COM_A4
  Repurchase          =~ C_REP1 + C_REP2 + C_REP3
  CoCreation          =~ C_CR1  + C_CR3  + C_CR4

  Satisfaction        ~ Image_Decoration + Image_Assortment + Image_Atmosphere +
                        Image_Luxury + Image_FrenchIdentity + Image_Gourmet + Image_Trendy
  AffectiveCommitment ~ Image_Decoration + Image_Assortment + Image_Atmosphere +
                        Image_Luxury + Image_FrenchIdentity + Image_Gourmet + Image_Trendy
  Repurchase          ~ Satisfaction + AffectiveCommitment
  CoCreation          ~ Satisfaction + AffectiveCommitment

  # Removed items as standalone predictors, not assigned to any factor
  Satisfaction        ~ Im8 + Im9 + Im11 + Im16 + Im19
  AffectiveCommitment ~ Im8 + Im9 + Im11 + Im16 + Im19
  Repurchase          ~ Im8 + Im9 + Im11 + Im16 + Im19
  CoCreation          ~ Im8 + Im9 + Im11 + Im16 + Im19
'
fit_robust <- cfa(sem_robust, data = data_raw, estimator = "MLR", missing = "fiml")
#compare fit measurements 
fit_robust_compare <- data.frame(
  Model   = c("Final SEM (clean items)",
              "Robustness SEM (+Im8/Im9/Im11/Im16/Im19 as predictors)"),
  Chi2_df = round(c(fitMeasures(fit_sem,    "chisq")/fitMeasures(fit_sem,    "df"),
                    fitMeasures(fit_robust, "chisq")/fitMeasures(fit_robust, "df")), 3),
  CFI     = round(c(fitMeasures(fit_sem,    "cfi"),   fitMeasures(fit_robust, "cfi")),   3),
  TLI     = round(c(fitMeasures(fit_sem,    "tli"),   fitMeasures(fit_robust, "tli")),   3),
  RMSEA   = round(c(fitMeasures(fit_sem,    "rmsea"), fitMeasures(fit_robust, "rmsea")), 3),
  SRMR    = round(c(fitMeasures(fit_sem,    "srmr"),  fitMeasures(fit_robust, "srmr")),  3)
)

fit_robust_compare %>%
  kbl(caption = "Robustness Check: Final SEM vs SEM with Removed Items as Predictors") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
  row_spec(1, bold = TRUE, background = "#d5f5e3")
Robustness Check: Final SEM vs SEM with Removed Items as Predictors
Model Chi2_df CFI TLI RMSEA SRMR
Final SEM (clean items) 1.693 0.977 0.973 0.035 0.041
Robustness SEM (+Im8/Im9/Im11/Im16/Im19 as predictors) 3.798 0.875 0.852 0.075 0.159

5.2 Robustness — Effect of Removed Items on Mediators (Im8, Im9, Im11, Im16, Im19)

removed_items <- c("Im8", "Im9", "Im11", "Im16", "Im19")

rob_med <- standardizedSolution(fit_robust) %>%
  filter(op == "~",
         lhs %in% c("Satisfaction", "AffectiveCommitment"),
         rhs %in% removed_items) %>%
  transmute(Outcome  = lhs,
            Item     = rhs,
            Beta     = round(est.std, 3),
            SE       = round(se, 3),
            z        = round(z, 2),
            p        = round(pvalue, 3),
            Sig      = ifelse(pvalue < .05, "Yes", "No"))

rob_med %>%
  kbl(caption = "Removed Items as Individual Predictors of Mediators",
      col.names = c("Mediator","Item","Beta","SE","z","p","Significant")) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
  column_spec(7, bold = TRUE,
              color = ifelse(rob_med$Sig == "Yes", "#c0392b", "#27ae60"))
Removed Items as Individual Predictors of Mediators
Mediator Item Beta SE z p Significant
Satisfaction Im8 0.060 0.088 0.69 0.491 No
Satisfaction Im9 -0.007 0.061 -0.12 0.902 No
Satisfaction Im11 0.027 0.059 0.46 0.646 No
Satisfaction Im16 0.071 0.079 0.90 0.369 No
Satisfaction Im19 0.231 0.069 3.37 0.001 Yes
AffectiveCommitment Im8 0.078 0.081 0.97 0.334 No
AffectiveCommitment Im9 0.024 0.059 0.41 0.684 No
AffectiveCommitment Im11 0.105 0.055 1.92 0.055 No
AffectiveCommitment Im16 -0.106 0.062 -1.71 0.088 No
AffectiveCommitment Im19 0.091 0.065 1.40 0.160 No

5.3 Robustness — Effect of Removed Items on Outcomes (Im8, Im9, Im11, Im16, Im19)

rob_out <- standardizedSolution(fit_robust) %>%
  filter(op == "~",
         lhs %in% c("Repurchase", "CoCreation"),
         rhs %in% removed_items) %>%
  transmute(Outcome  = lhs,
            Item     = rhs,
            Beta     = round(est.std, 3),
            SE       = round(se, 3),
            z        = round(z, 2),
            p        = round(pvalue, 3),
            Sig      = ifelse(pvalue < .05, "Yes", "No"))

rob_out %>%
  kbl(caption = "Removed Items as Individual Predictors of Outcomes",
      col.names = c("Outcome","Item","Beta","SE","z","p","Significant")) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
  column_spec(7, bold = TRUE,
              color = ifelse(rob_out$Sig == "Yes", "#c0392b", "#27ae60"))
Removed Items as Individual Predictors of Outcomes
Outcome Item Beta SE z p Significant
Repurchase Im8 0.075 0.056 1.33 0.182 No
Repurchase Im9 -0.093 0.054 -1.71 0.087 No
Repurchase Im11 0.096 0.046 2.06 0.039 Yes
Repurchase Im16 -0.049 0.055 -0.90 0.366 No
Repurchase Im19 0.068 0.056 1.22 0.223 No
CoCreation Im8 -0.056 0.053 -1.04 0.299 No
CoCreation Im9 -0.022 0.061 -0.36 0.718 No
CoCreation Im11 0.066 0.049 1.35 0.178 No
CoCreation Im16 -0.048 0.066 -0.73 0.466 No
CoCreation Im19 0.012 0.067 0.18 0.859 No 
bind_rows(rob_med, rob_out) %>%
  mutate(Alpha = ifelse(Sig == "Yes", 1, 0.35),
         Target = Outcome) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Item, y = Beta, fill = Target, alpha = I(Alpha))) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(0.8), width = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "grey40") +
  facet_wrap(~Target, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2", guide = "none") +
  labs(title    = "Robustness: Direct Effects of Removed Items",
       subtitle = "Transparent = non-significant (p > .05) | Significant = potential bias",
       x = "Removed Item", y = "Standardised Beta") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"))

The chart shows the direct effects of the five removed items (Im11, Im16, Im19, Im8, Im9) on each of the four endogenous constructs, with transparent bars indicating non-significance.

Im19 stands out as the most problematic item. It has a large, significant direct effect on Satisfaction (beta ≈ 0.22), suggesting it carries unique predictive information not captured by the seven-factor structure.

Im11 is the second most noteworthy item, showing significant direct effects on Repurchase (beta ≈ 0.10), but compared to Im19, its p-value is larger (0.039).

5.4 Sensitivity to Removed Items : Im19

The robustness analysis found that Im19 (“Professional Organisation”) has a significant direct effect on Satisfaction when added as a standalone predictor. Before incorporating it into the final model, we test whether doing so actually improves model fit.

fit_ext_compare <- data.frame(
  Model   = c("Final SEM (7 factors only)",
              "Extended SEM (+Im19, +direct paths)"),
  Chi2_df = round(c(fitMeasures(fit_sem,      "chisq")/fitMeasures(fit_sem,      "df"),
                    fitMeasures(fit_extended, "chisq")/fitMeasures(fit_extended, "df")), 3),
  CFI     = round(c(fitMeasures(fit_sem,      "cfi"),   fitMeasures(fit_extended, "cfi")),   3),
  RMSEA   = round(c(fitMeasures(fit_sem,      "rmsea"), fitMeasures(fit_extended, "rmsea")), 3),
  SRMR    = round(c(fitMeasures(fit_sem,      "srmr"),  fitMeasures(fit_extended, "srmr")),  3),
  AIC     = round(c(fitMeasures(fit_sem,      "aic"),   fitMeasures(fit_extended, "aic")),   1)
)

fit_ext_compare %>%
  kbl(caption = "Fit Comparison: Final SEM vs Extended SEM with Im19") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
  row_spec(1, bold = TRUE, background = "#d5f5e3")
Fit Comparison: Final SEM vs Extended SEM with Im19
Model Chi2_df CFI RMSEA SRMR AIC
Final SEM (7 factors only) 1.693 0.977 0.035 0.041 42159.5
Extended SEM (+Im19, +direct paths) 2.607 0.946 0.054 0.098 41391.2

Despite Im19 showing a significant path to Satisfaction in isolation, adding it (alongside direct paths from image dimensions to outcomes) substantially worsens model fit: CFI decreases from 0.977 to 0.946, RMSEA rises from 0.035 to 0.054, and SRMR nearly doubles from 0.041 to 0.098. Although AIC is lower in the extended model (reflecting the additional free parameters), the degradation in CFI, RMSEA and SRMR indicates that the added complexity hurts overall model structure more than it helps. The original full mediation SEM is therefore retained as the final model.

Among the five items tested in the robustness model, Im19 (“Professional Organisation”) is the only one that shows a significant direct effect on Satisfaction (sections 5.2–5.3). But despite the significant path, adding Im19 alongside direct image-to-outcome paths substantially worsens model fit, and the original full mediation SEM is retained.

Appendix

Declaration on the Use of Generative AI Tools

AI tools were used during the preparation of this report to improve workflow efficiency, particularly for coding support and for formatting tables, graphics, and results in the HTML output. Claude was consulted to assist with technical implementation and visual presentation.

The analysis itself, including the choice of methods, interpretation of findings, and final conclusions, was carried out independently by the authors.
